                'Data processing, hosting, and related services'
              )
           OR "industry_group" = 'Computer systems design and related services'
          )
),

-- 2. Google overall hiring percentages for 2021
google_2021 AS (
    SELECT
        "race_white"           AS google_white ,
        "race_black"           AS google_black ,
        "race_hispanic_latinx" AS google_hispanic ,
        "race_asian"           AS google_asian
    FROM GOOGLE_DEI.GOOGLE_DEI.DAR_NON_INTERSECTIONAL_HIRING
    WHERE "workforce" = 'overall'
      AND "report_year" = 2021
)

-- 3. Compute absolute differences for each race and return top three
SELECT
    race,
    ROUND(diff, 4) AS difference
FROM (
    SELECT 
        'Asian'                         AS race,
        ABS(g.google_asian   - b.bls_asian)    AS diff
    FROM google_2021 g, bls_tech_2021 b

    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        'Black or African American'     AS race,
        ABS(g.google_black   - b.bls_black)    AS diff
    FROM google_2021 g, bls_tech_2021 b

    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        'Hispanic or Latino'            AS race,
        ABS(g.google_hispanic - b.bls_hispanic) AS diff
    FROM google_2021 g, bls_tech_2021 b

    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        'White'                         AS race,
        ABS(g.google_white   - b.bls_white)    AS diff
    FROM google_2021 g, bls_tech_2021 b
) differences
ORDER BY difference DESC
LIMIT 3;

question: Could you provide a combined 2021 report comparing racial (Asian, Black, Hispanic/Latinx, White) and gender (U.S. Women, U.S. Men) distributions across Google’s overall workforce hiring, Google’s overall workforce representation, and the BLS data specifically for the technology sectors defined as Internet publishing and broadcasting and web search portals or Computer systems design and related services?
SQL:
/* 2021 racial & gender distribution comparison
   – Google Overall Workforce Representation (2021)
   – Google Overall Workforce Hiring (2021)
   – BLS tech-sector data (2021) for
       • Internet publishing and broadcasting and web search portals
       • Computer systems design and related services
*/
WITH
-- 1) Google overall workforce representation (2021)
google_rep AS (
    SELECT
        'Google – 2021 Representation'          AS "dataset",
        "race_asian"                            AS "race_asian",
        "race_black"                            AS "race_black",
        "race_hispanic_latinx"                  AS "race_hispanic_latinx",
        "race_white"                            AS "race_white",
        "gender_us_women"                       AS "gender_women",
        "gender_us_men"                         AS "gender_men"
    FROM GOOGLE_DEI.GOOGLE_DEI.DAR_NON_INTERSECTIONAL_REPRESENTATION
    WHERE "workforce" = 'overall'
      AND "report_year" = 2021
),

-- 2) Google overall workforce hiring (2021)
google_hire AS (
    SELECT
        'Google – 2021 Hiring'                  AS "dataset",
        "race_asian",
        "race_black",
        "race_hispanic_latinx",
        "race_white",
        "gender_us_women"                       AS "gender_women",
        "gender_us_men"                         AS "gender_men"
    FROM GOOGLE_DEI.GOOGLE_DEI.DAR_NON_INTERSECTIONAL_HIRING
    WHERE "workforce" = 'overall'
      AND "report_year" = 2021
),

-- 3) BLS tech-sector data (Internet publishing & Computer systems design)
bls AS (
    SELECT
        CASE
            WHEN COALESCE("industry", "industry_group", "subsector") = 'Internet publishing and broadcasting and web search portals'
                 THEN 'BLS – Internet Publishing/Broadcasting & Web Search Portals (2021)'
            ELSE 'BLS – Computer Systems Design & Related Services (2021)'
        END                                           AS "dataset",
        "percent_asian"                               AS "race_asian",
        "percent_black_or_african_american"           AS "race_black",
        "percent_hispanic_or_latino"                  AS "race_hispanic_latinx",
        "percent_white"                               AS "race_white",
        "percent_women"                               AS "gender_women",
        (1 - "percent_women")                         AS "gender_men"
    FROM GOOGLE_DEI.BLS.CPSAAT18
    WHERE "year" = 2021
      AND (
            "subsector"      IN ('Internet publishing and broadcasting and web search portals',
                                 'Computer systems design and related services')
         OR "industry_group" IN ('Internet publishing and broadcasting and web search portals',
                                 'Computer systems design and related services')
         OR "industry"       IN ('Internet publishing and broadcasting and web search portals',
                                 'Computer systems design and related services')
      )
)

-- Combine all data sets
SELECT
    "dataset",
    ROUND("race_asian",               4) AS "pct_asian",
    ROUND("race_black",               4) AS "pct_black",
    ROUND("race_hispanic_latinx",     4) AS "pct_hispanic_latinx",
    ROUND("race_white",               4) AS "pct_white",
    ROUND("gender_women",             4) AS "pct_us_women",
    ROUND("gender_men",               4) AS "pct_us_men"
FROM (
    SELECT * FROM google_rep
    UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM google_hire
    UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM bls
) AS combined
ORDER BY "dataset";

question: Please calculate the growth rates for Asians, Black people, Latinx people, Native Americans, White people, US women, US men, global women, and global men from 2014 to 2024 concerning the overall workforce.
SQL:
WITH base AS (
    /* 1.  Select overall–workforce records for the two years of interest              */
    SELECT
        "report_year",
        "race_asian",
        "race_black",
        "race_hispanic_latinx",
        "race_native_american",
        "race_white",
        "gender_us_women",
        "gender_us_men",
        "gender_global_women",
        "gender_global_men"
    FROM GOOGLE_DEI.GOOGLE_DEI."DAR_NON_INTERSECTIONAL_REPRESENTATION"
    WHERE "workforce" = 'overall'
      AND "report_year" IN (2014, 2024)
),
/* 2.  Turn the wide table into two-column (metric, value) format, keeping year        */
unpivoted AS (
    SELECT
        "report_year",
        metric,
        val
    FROM base
    UNPIVOT(val FOR metric IN (
        "race_asian",
        "race_black",
        "race_hispanic_latinx",
        "race_native_american",
        "race_white",
        "gender_us_women",
        "gender_us_men",
        "gender_global_women",
        "gender_global_men"
    ))
),
/* 3.  Separate 2014 and 2024 values for each metric                                   */
pivoted AS (
    SELECT
        metric,
        MAX(CASE WHEN "report_year" = 2014 THEN val END) AS value_2014,
        MAX(CASE WHEN "report_year" = 2024 THEN val END) AS value_2024
    FROM unpivoted
    GROUP BY metric
)
/* 4.  Compute growth rate = ((2024 – 2014) / 2014) * 100                              */
SELECT
    metric,
    ROUND(value_2014, 4)   AS "value_2014",
    ROUND(value_2024, 4)   AS "value_2024",
    ROUND(
        (value_2024 - value_2014) / NULLIF(value_2014, 0) * 100,
        4
    )                      AS "growth_rate_pct"
FROM pivoted
ORDER BY metric;

question: For each month in the year 2023, how many total transactions occurred (counting all transaction records without removing duplicates of transaction hashes), and how many transactions per second were processed each month, where the transactions-per-second value is calculated by dividing the monthly total count by the exact number of seconds in that month, including the correct leap-year logic if applicable based on the extracted year from the transaction timestamp? Show the monthly transaction count, the computed transactions per second, the year, and the month, and present the rows in descending order of the monthly transaction count.
SQL:
WITH ALL_TRANSACTIONS AS (   
    /* Collect every transaction record (duplicates kept) from each chain’s
       TRANSACTIONS tables that contain 2023 timestamps                       */
    SELECT  "block_timestamp"
    FROM    GOOG_BLOCKCHAIN.GOOG_BLOCKCHAIN_CRONOS_MAINNET_US."TRANSACTIONS"
    UNION ALL
    SELECT  "block_timestamp"
    FROM    GOOG_BLOCKCHAIN.GOOG_BLOCKCHAIN_POLYGON_MAINNET_US."TRANSACTIONS"
)
, MONTHLY_COUNTS AS (    
    /* Aggregate by month for year-2023 */
    SELECT
        DATE_TRUNC('month', "block_timestamp")     AS month_start ,
        COUNT(*)                                   AS monthly_tx_count
    FROM    ALL_TRANSACTIONS
    WHERE   YEAR("block_timestamp") = 2023
    GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', "block_timestamp")
)
SELECT
    mc.monthly_tx_count                                           AS "MONTH_TX_COUNT",
    ROUND(
        mc.monthly_tx_count 
        / NULLIF(
              DATEDIFF(
                  'second',
                  mc.month_start,
                  DATEADD('month', 1, mc.month_start)          -- first second of next month
              )
          ,0)
    , 4)                                                          AS "TPS",
    YEAR(mc.month_start)                                          AS "YEAR",
    MONTH(mc.month_start)                                         AS "MONTH"
FROM    MONTHLY_COUNTS mc
ORDER BY mc.monthly_tx_count DESC;

question: Which sender address, represented as a complete URL on https://cronoscan.com, has been used most frequently on the Cronos blockchain in transactions to non-null 'to_address' fields, within blocks larger than 4096 bytes, since January 1, 2023?
SQL:
SELECT CONCAT('https://cronoscan.com/address/', t."from_address") AS sender_url
FROM GOOG_BLOCKCHAIN.GOOG_BLOCKCHAIN_CRONOS_MAINNET_US.TRANSACTIONS t
JOIN GOOG_BLOCKCHAIN.GOOG_BLOCKCHAIN_CRONOS_MAINNET_US.BLOCKS b
  ON t."block_hash" = b."block_hash"
WHERE t."to_address" IS NOT NULL
  AND b."size" > 4096
  AND b."block_timestamp" >= '2023-01-01'
GROUP BY sender_url
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Considering only the highest release versions of NPM packages, which dependency (package and its version) appears most frequently among the dependencies of these packages?
SQL:
WITH highest_release AS (
    /* For every NPM package keep the highest (latest) *released* version        */
    SELECT
        "Name",
        "Version"
    FROM (
        SELECT
            "Name",
            "Version",
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
                PARTITION BY "Name"
                ORDER BY ("VersionInfo":"Ordinal")::NUMBER DESC      /* latest */
            ) AS rn
        FROM DEPS_DEV_V1.DEPS_DEV_V1.PACKAGEVERSIONS
        WHERE "System" = 'NPM'
          AND ("VersionInfo":"IsRelease")::BOOLEAN = TRUE            /* releases only */
    )
    WHERE rn = 1
),
deps_per_pkg AS (
    /* Dependencies declared by those highest-release package versions          */
    SELECT DISTINCT
        d."Name"          AS pkg_name,
        d."Version"       AS pkg_version,
        d."Dependency":"Name"::TEXT    AS dep_name,
        d."Dependency":"Version"::TEXT AS dep_version
    FROM DEPS_DEV_V1.DEPS_DEV_V1.DEPENDENCIES d
    JOIN highest_release hr
          ON d."System" = 'NPM'               /* parent package is NPM          */
         AND d."Name"   = hr."Name"
         AND d."Version"= hr."Version"
)
SELECT
    dep_name   AS "DependencyName",
    dep_version AS "DependencyVersion",
    COUNT(*)   AS "AppearanceCount"
FROM deps_per_pkg
GROUP BY dep_name, dep_version
ORDER BY "AppearanceCount" DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Find the GitHub URL (with link label 'SOURCE_REPO') of the latest released version of the NPM package that has the highest number of dependencies in its latest released version, excluding packages whose names contain the character '@' and only considering URLs where the link label is 'SOURCE_REPO' and the URL contains 'github.com'.
SQL:
WITH ReleasedVersions AS (
    SELECT
        "Name",
        "Version",
        "SnapshotAt"
    FROM
        DEPS_DEV_V1.DEPS_DEV_V1.PACKAGEVERSIONS
    WHERE
        "System" = 'NPM'
        AND "Name" NOT LIKE '%@%'
        AND PARSE_JSON("VersionInfo"):IsRelease = true
),
LatestReleasedVersions AS (
    SELECT
        rv."Name",
        rv."Version",
        rv."SnapshotAt"
    FROM
        ReleasedVersions rv
    INNER JOIN (
        SELECT
            "Name",
            MAX("SnapshotAt") AS "MaxSnapshotAt"
        FROM
            ReleasedVersions
        GROUP BY
            "Name"
    ) lrv_max
        ON rv."Name" = lrv_max."Name" AND rv."SnapshotAt" = lrv_max."MaxSnapshotAt"
),
DependencyCounts AS (
    SELECT
        lv."Name",
        lv."Version",
        lv."SnapshotAt",
        COUNT(d."Dependency") AS "NumDependencies"
    FROM
        LatestReleasedVersions lv
    LEFT JOIN DEPS_DEV_V1.DEPS_DEV_V1.DEPENDENCIES d
        ON lv."Name" = d."Name"
        AND lv."Version" = d."Version"
        AND lv."SnapshotAt" = d."SnapshotAt"
        AND d."System" = 'NPM'
    GROUP BY
        lv."Name",
        lv."Version",
        lv."SnapshotAt"
),
SourceRepoLinks AS (
    SELECT
        pv."Name",
        pv."Version",
        l.value:"URL"::STRING AS "SOURCE_REPO_URL"
    FROM
        DEPS_DEV_V1.DEPS_DEV_V1.PACKAGEVERSIONS pv,
        LATERAL FLATTEN(INPUT => TRY_PARSE_JSON(pv."Links")) l
    WHERE
        pv."System" = 'NPM'
        AND pv."Name" NOT LIKE '%@%'
        AND l.value:"Label"::STRING = 'SOURCE_REPO'
        AND l.value:"URL"::STRING ILIKE '%github.com%'
        AND PARSE_JSON(pv."VersionInfo"):IsRelease = true
),
Candidates AS (
    SELECT
        dc."Name",
        dc."Version",
        dc."NumDependencies",
        srl."SOURCE_REPO_URL"
    FROM
        DependencyCounts dc
    JOIN SourceRepoLinks srl
        ON dc."Name" = srl."Name"
        AND dc."Version" = srl."Version"
)
SELECT
    "SOURCE_REPO_URL"
FROM
    Candidates
QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY "NumDependencies" DESC, "Name" ASC) = 1
LIMIT 1;

question: Considering only the latest release versions of NPM package, which packages are the top 8 most popular based on the Github star number, as well as their versions?
SQL:
SELECT
    q."package_name",
    q."version",
    q."github_stars"
FROM (
    WITH latest_release AS (
        SELECT
            pv."Name",
            pv."Version"
        FROM "DEPS_DEV_V1"."DEPS_DEV_V1"."PACKAGEVERSIONS" pv
        WHERE pv."System" = 'NPM'
          AND pv."Name" NOT LIKE '%>%'
          AND COALESCE((pv."VersionInfo":"IsRelease")::BOOLEAN, FALSE)
        QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY pv."Name"
            ORDER BY COALESCE(pv."UpstreamPublishedAt", pv."SnapshotAt") DESC, pv."Version" DESC
        ) = 1
    ),
    latest_project AS (
        SELECT
            pr."Name" AS "ProjectName",
            pr."StarsCount"
        FROM "DEPS_DEV_V1"."DEPS_DEV_V1"."PROJECTS" pr
        WHERE pr."Type" = 'GITHUB'
        QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY pr."Name"
            ORDER BY pr."SnapshotAt" DESC
        ) = 1
    ),
    package_project AS (
        SELECT DISTINCT
            pvtp."Name",
            pvtp."Version",
            pvtp."ProjectName"
        FROM "DEPS_DEV_V1"."DEPS_DEV_V1"."PACKAGEVERSIONTOPROJECT" pvtp
        WHERE pvtp."System" = 'NPM'
          AND pvtp."ProjectType" = 'GITHUB'
          AND pvtp."RelationType" = 'SOURCE_REPO_TYPE'
    )
    SELECT
        lr."Name" AS "package_name",
        lr."Version" AS "version",
        lp."StarsCount" AS "github_stars",
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY lr."Name"
            ORDER BY lp."StarsCount" DESC, mp."ProjectName"
        ) AS rn
    FROM latest_release lr
    JOIN package_project mp
      ON mp."Name" = lr."Name"
     AND mp."Version" = lr."Version"
    JOIN latest_project lp
      ON lp."ProjectName" = mp."ProjectName"
) q
WHERE q.rn = 1
ORDER BY q."github_stars" DESC, q."package_name"
LIMIT 8

question: Which three companies had the largest increase in trip numbers between two consecutive months in 2018?
SQL:
WITH trips_2018 AS (
    -- Keep only 2018 rows using the micro-second epoch range for 2018
    SELECT
        "company",
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_TIMESTAMP_LTZ("trip_start_timestamp" / 1000000)) AS trip_month
    FROM CHICAGO.CHICAGO_TAXI_TRIPS.TAXI_TRIPS
    WHERE "trip_start_timestamp" >= 1514764800000000      -- 2018-01-01 00:00:00 UTC
      AND "trip_start_timestamp" <  1546300800000000      -- 2019-01-01 00:00:00 UTC
      AND "company" IS NOT NULL
),
monthly_counts AS (
    -- Trip counts per company per month
    SELECT
        "company",
        trip_month,
        COUNT(*) AS trips_in_month
    FROM trips_2018
    GROUP BY "company", trip_month
),
monthly_diffs AS (
    -- Month-over-month differences
    SELECT
        "company",
        trip_month,
        trips_in_month
            - LAG(trips_in_month) OVER (PARTITION BY "company" ORDER BY trip_month) AS diff_from_prev
    FROM monthly_counts
),
max_positive_increase AS (
    -- Best (largest) positive increase per company
    SELECT
        "company",
        MAX(diff_from_prev) AS max_monthly_increase
    FROM monthly_diffs
    WHERE diff_from_prev > 0
    GROUP BY "company"
)
-- Top 3 companies with the largest single-month increase
SELECT
    "company"
FROM max_positive_increase
ORDER BY max_monthly_increase DESC
LIMIT 3;

question: What is the highest number of motor vehicle theft incidents that occurred in any single month during 2016?
SQL:
SELECT EXTRACT(MONTH FROM TO_TIMESTAMP("date"/1000000)) AS "month", COUNT(*) AS "motor_vehicle_theft_count"
FROM CHICAGO.CHICAGO_CRIME.CRIME
WHERE "year" = 2016 AND "primary_type" = 'MOTOR VEHICLE THEFT'
GROUP BY "month"
ORDER BY "motor_vehicle_theft_count" DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: For each year from 2010 to 2016, what is the highest number of motor thefts in one month?
SQL:
SELECT
    "year",
    MAX(monthly_thefts) AS max_monthly_thefts
FROM (
    SELECT
        "year",
        EXTRACT(MONTH FROM TO_TIMESTAMP_NTZ("date" / 1e6)) AS month,
        COUNT(*) AS monthly_thefts
    FROM
        CHICAGO.CHICAGO_CRIME.CRIME
    WHERE
        "year" BETWEEN 2010 AND 2016
        AND "primary_type" = 'MOTOR VEHICLE THEFT'
    GROUP BY
        "year",
        month
)
GROUP BY
    "year"
ORDER BY
    "year";

question: For the detailed molecule data, Please display the drug id, drug type and withdrawal status for approved drugs with a black box warning and known drug type among 'Keytruda', 'Vioxx', 'Premarin', and 'Humira'
SQL:
SELECT "id", "drugType", "hasBeenWithdrawn"
FROM OPEN_TARGETS_PLATFORM_1.PLATFORM.MOLECULE
WHERE "isApproved" = TRUE
  AND "blackBoxWarning" = TRUE
  AND LOWER("name") IN ('pembrolizumab', 'rofecoxib', 'conjugated estrogens', 'adalimumab')
  AND "drugType" IS NOT NULL;

question: Which target approved symbol has the overall association score closest to the mean score for psoriasis?
SQL:
WITH psoriasis_scores AS (
    SELECT
        "score",
        "targetId"
    FROM
        OPEN_TARGETS_PLATFORM_1.PLATFORM.ASSOCIATIONBYOVERALLDIRECT
    WHERE
        "diseaseId" = 'EFO_0000676'
),
mean_score_cte AS (
    SELECT AVG("score") AS mean_score FROM psoriasis_scores
),
closest AS (
    SELECT
        ps."targetId",
        ps."score",
        ABS(ps."score" - m.mean_score) AS diff
    FROM
        psoriasis_scores ps
    CROSS JOIN
        mean_score_cte m
    ORDER BY diff ASC
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
    t."approvedSymbol" AS target_approved_symbol,
    c."score" AS overall_association_score,
    (SELECT mean_score FROM mean_score_cte) AS mean_score_for_psoriasis
FROM
    closest c
JOIN
    OPEN_TARGETS_PLATFORM_1.PLATFORM.TARGETS t
    ON c."targetId" = t."id";

question: Retrieve the approved symbol of target genes with the highest overall score that are associated with the disease 'EFO_0000676' from the data source 'IMPC'.
SQL:
WITH impc_targets AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT "targetId"
    FROM OPEN_TARGETS_PLATFORM_2.OPEN_TARGETS_PLATFORM."ASSOCIATIONBYDATASOURCEDIRECT"
    WHERE "diseaseId" = 'EFO_0000676'
      AND LOWER("datasourceId") = 'impc'
),
scores AS (
    SELECT od."targetId",
           od."score"
    FROM OPEN_TARGETS_PLATFORM_2.OPEN_TARGETS_PLATFORM."ASSOCIATIONBYOVERALLDIRECT" od
    JOIN impc_targets it
      ON od."targetId" = it."targetId"
    WHERE od."diseaseId" = 'EFO_0000676'
),
max_score AS (
    SELECT MAX("score") AS "max_score"
    FROM scores
)
SELECT DISTINCT t."approvedSymbol"
FROM scores s
JOIN max_score m
  ON s."score" = m."max_score"
JOIN OPEN_TARGETS_PLATFORM_2.OPEN_TARGETS_PLATFORM."TARGETS" t
  ON t."id" = s."targetId"
ORDER BY t."approvedSymbol";

question: Generate a list of drugs from the table containing molecular details that have completed clinical trials for pancreatic endocrine carcinoma, disease ID EFO_0007416. Please include each drug's name, the target approved symbol, and links to the relevant clinical trials.
SQL:
SELECT 
    k."prefName" AS "Drug Name",
    t."approvedSymbol" AS "Target Approved Symbol",
    k."urls" AS "Clinical Trial Links"
FROM 
    OPEN_TARGETS_PLATFORM_1.PLATFORM.KNOWNDRUGSAGGREGATED k
JOIN 
    OPEN_TARGETS_PLATFORM_1.PLATFORM.TARGETS t
ON 
    k."targetId" = t."id"
WHERE 
    k."diseaseId" = 'EFO_0007416' 
    AND LOWER(k."status") = 'completed';

question: Find the average, variance, max-min difference, and the QTL source(right study) of the maximum log2(h4/h3) for data where right gene id is "ENSG00000169174", h4 > 0.8, h3 < 0.02, reported trait includes "lesterol levels", right biological feature is "IPSC", and the variant is '1_55029009_C_T'.
SQL:
WITH filt AS (
    SELECT 
        vdc."coloc_log2_h4_h3",
        vdc."right_study"
    FROM OPEN_TARGETS_GENETICS_1.GENETICS.VARIANT_DISEASE_COLOC vdc
    JOIN OPEN_TARGETS_GENETICS_1.GENETICS.STUDIES s
        ON vdc."left_study" = s."study_id"
    WHERE vdc."right_gene_id"     = 'ENSG00000169174'
      AND vdc."coloc_h4"          > 0.8
      AND vdc."coloc_h3"          < 0.02
      AND vdc."right_bio_feature" = 'IPSC'
      AND CONCAT(vdc."left_chrom", '_', TO_VARCHAR(vdc."left_pos"), '_', vdc."left_ref", '_', vdc."left_alt") = '1_55029009_C_T'
      AND LOWER(s."trait_reported") LIKE '%lesterol levels%'
),
stats AS (
    SELECT
        AVG("coloc_log2_h4_h3") AS avg_log2_h4_h3,
        VAR_SAMP("coloc_log2_h4_h3") AS var_log2_h4_h3,
        MAX("coloc_log2_h4_h3") - MIN("coloc_log2_h4_h3") AS max_min_diff
    FROM filt
),
best AS (
    SELECT 
        "right_study" AS qtl_source
    FROM filt
    ORDER BY "coloc_log2_h4_h3" DESC
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
    stats.avg_log2_h4_h3,
    stats.var_log2_h4_h3,
    stats.max_min_diff,
    best.qtl_source
FROM stats
CROSS JOIN best;

question: Please identify the top 10 genes with the strongest associations across all studies by first selecting, for each gene within each study, the variant with the lowest p-value, and then ranking all such gene–variant pairs to return the 10 genes with the smallest p-values overall.
SQL:
WITH gene_variant_pvalues AS (
    SELECT 
        "gene_id", 
        "study_id", 
        "pval"
    FROM OPEN_TARGETS_GENETICS_2.OPEN_TARGETS_GENETICS.DISEASE_VARIANT_GENE
    WHERE "pval" IS NOT NULL
    LIMIT 10000  -- Limiting rows to ensure quick processing
),
ranked_gene_variants AS (
    SELECT 
        "gene_id", 
        "study_id",
        "pval",
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY "gene_id", "study_id" ORDER BY "pval" ASC) AS rn
    FROM gene_variant_pvalues
),
best_gene_variants AS (
    SELECT 
        "gene_id", 
        MIN("pval") AS "best_pval"
    FROM ranked_gene_variants
    WHERE rn=1
    GROUP BY "gene_id"
)
SELECT 
    "gene_id", 
    "best_pval"
FROM best_gene_variants
ORDER BY "best_pval" ASC
LIMIT 10;

question: Please collect the information of the top 6 trade report with the highest closing prices. Refer to the document for all the information I want.
SQL:
SELECT
    "OrderID" AS tradeID,
    TO_TIMESTAMP("MaturityDate" / 1000000) AS tradeTimestamp,
    CASE 
        WHEN SUBSTRING("TargetCompID", 1, 4) = 'MOMO' THEN 'Momentum'
        WHEN SUBSTRING("TargetCompID", 1, 4) = 'LUCK' THEN 'Feeling Lucky'
        WHEN SUBSTRING("TargetCompID", 1, 4) = 'PRED' THEN 'Prediction'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
    END AS algorithm,
    "Symbol" AS symbol,
    "LastPx" AS openPrice,
    "StrikePrice" AS closePrice,
    side.value:"Side"::STRING AS tradeDirection,
    CASE 
        WHEN side.value:"Side"::STRING = 'SHORT' THEN -1
        WHEN side.value:"Side"::STRING = 'LONG' THEN 1
        ELSE 0
    END AS tradeMultiplier
FROM CYMBAL_INVESTMENTS.CYMBAL_INVESTMENTS.TRADE_CAPTURE_REPORT,
     LATERAL FLATTEN(input => TRY_PARSE_JSON("Sides")) AS side
ORDER BY closePrice DESC
LIMIT 6;

question: Considering only the latest evaluation group per state for the 'EXPCURR' evaluation type, determine which state has the highest total acreage of timberland and which has the highest total acreage of forestland. For timberland, include plots where the condition status code is 1, the reserved status code is 0, and the site productivity class code is between 1 and 6. For forestland, include plots where the condition status code is 1. Calculate the total acres by summing the adjusted expansion factors for macroplots and subplots, using their respective proportion bases ('MACR' for macroplots and 'SUBP' for subplots) and adjustment factors when greater than zero. For each category (timberland and forestland), provide the state code, evaluation group, state name, and the total acres for the state with the highest total acreage, considering only the latest evaluation group per state.
SQL:
WITH latest_eval_group_forests AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT efa."state_code", 
           efa."evaluation_group", 
           efa."state_name"
    FROM USFS_FIA.USFS_FIA.ESTIMATED_FORESTLAND_ACRES efa
    JOIN (
        SELECT "state_code", MAX("evaluation_group") AS max_eval_group
        FROM USFS_FIA.USFS_FIA.ESTIMATED_FORESTLAND_ACRES
        WHERE "evaluation_type" = 'EXPCURR'
        GROUP BY "state_code"
    ) le 
      ON efa."state_code" = le."state_code" 
     AND efa."evaluation_group" = le.max_eval_group
    WHERE efa."evaluation_type" = 'EXPCURR'
),
latest_eval_group_timber AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT eta."state_code", 
           eta."evaluation_group", 
           eta."state_name"
    FROM USFS_FIA.USFS_FIA.ESTIMATED_TIMBERLAND_ACRES eta
    JOIN (
        SELECT "state_code", MAX("evaluation_group") AS max_eval_group
        FROM USFS_FIA.USFS_FIA.ESTIMATED_TIMBERLAND_ACRES
        WHERE "evaluation_type" = 'EXPCURR'
        GROUP BY "state_code"
    ) le 
      ON eta."state_code" = le."state_code" 
     AND eta."evaluation_group" = le.max_eval_group
    WHERE eta."evaluation_type" = 'EXPCURR'
),
timberland_acres AS (
    SELECT lt."state_code",
           lt."evaluation_group",
           lt."state_name",
           SUM(
               CASE 
                   WHEN c."proportion_basis" = 'MACR' 
                        AND pop."adjustment_factor_for_the_macroplot" > 0 
                   THEN eta."macroplot_acres" * pop."adjustment_factor_for_the_macroplot"
                   WHEN c."proportion_basis" = 'SUBP'
                        AND pop."adjustment_factor_for_the_subplot" > 0
                   THEN eta."subplot_acres" * pop."adjustment_factor_for_the_subplot"
                   ELSE 0
               END
           ) AS total_acres
    FROM latest_eval_group_timber lt
    JOIN USFS_FIA.USFS_FIA.ESTIMATED_TIMBERLAND_ACRES eta
      ON lt."state_code" = eta."state_code"
     AND lt."evaluation_group" = eta."evaluation_group"
    JOIN USFS_FIA.USFS_FIA.POPULATION pop
      ON eta."plot_sequence_number" = pop."plot_sequence_number"
     AND lt."evaluation_group" = pop."evaluation_group"
    JOIN USFS_FIA.USFS_FIA.CONDITION c
      ON eta."plot_sequence_number" = c."plot_sequence_number"
    WHERE c."condition_status_code" = 1
      AND c."reserved_status_code" = 0
      AND c."site_productivity_class_code" BETWEEN 1 AND 6
    GROUP BY lt."state_code", lt."evaluation_group", lt."state_name"
),
forestland_acres AS (
    SELECT lf."state_code",
           lf."evaluation_group",
           lf."state_name",
           SUM(
               CASE 
                   WHEN c."proportion_basis" = 'MACR' 
                        AND pop."adjustment_factor_for_the_macroplot" > 0 
                   THEN efa."macroplot_acres" * pop."adjustment_factor_for_the_macroplot"
                   WHEN c."proportion_basis" = 'SUBP'
                        AND pop."adjustment_factor_for_the_subplot" > 0
                   THEN efa."subplot_acres" * pop."adjustment_factor_for_the_subplot"
                   ELSE 0
               END
           ) AS total_acres
    FROM latest_eval_group_forests lf
    JOIN USFS_FIA.USFS_FIA.ESTIMATED_FORESTLAND_ACRES efa
      ON lf."state_code" = efa."state_code"
     AND lf."evaluation_group" = efa."evaluation_group"
    JOIN USFS_FIA.USFS_FIA.POPULATION pop
      ON efa."plot_sequence_number" = pop."plot_sequence_number"
     AND lf."evaluation_group" = pop."evaluation_group"
    JOIN USFS_FIA.USFS_FIA.CONDITION c
      ON efa."plot_sequence_number" = c."plot_sequence_number"
    WHERE c."condition_status_code" = 1
    GROUP BY lf."state_code", lf."evaluation_group", lf."state_name"
),
top_timber AS (
    SELECT 'Timberland' AS category,
           ta."state_code", 
           ta."evaluation_group", 
           ta."state_name", 
           ta.total_acres
    FROM timberland_acres ta
    ORDER BY ta.total_acres DESC
    LIMIT 1
),
top_forest AS (
    SELECT 'Forestland' AS category,
           fa."state_code", 
           fa."evaluation_group", 
           fa."state_name", 
           fa.total_acres
    FROM forestland_acres fa
    ORDER BY fa.total_acres DESC
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT * FROM top_timber
UNION ALL
SELECT * FROM top_forest;

question: Based on the condition, plot_tree, and population tables in bigquery-public-data.usfs_fia, for the evaluation_type set to 'EXPCURR' and condition_status_code equal to 1, which states had the largest average subplot size and the largest average macroplot size, respectively, for each of the years 2015, 2016, and 2017? Please include the type of plot (subplot or macroplot), the specific year, the state, and the corresponding average size in your results.
SQL:
WITH base AS (
    /*--------------------------------------------------------------------
      Join CONDITION (plot/condition level) with POPULATION (expansion &
      adjustment factors) for the CURRENT-AREA evaluation (EXPCURR).
      Keep forest-land conditions only (CONDITION_STATUS_CD = 1) and the
      three study years 2015-2017.
    --------------------------------------------------------------------*/
    SELECT
        c."inventory_year"                                            AS "year",
        c."state_code"                                                AS "state_code",
        c."state_code_name"                                           AS "state_name",
        /* subplot size (only where proportion basis = 'SUBP') */
        CASE
            WHEN c."proportion_basis" = 'SUBP'
                 AND p."adjustment_factor_for_the_subplot" > 0
            THEN  p."expansion_factor"
                * c."condition_proportion_unadjusted"
                * p."adjustment_factor_for_the_subplot"
        END                                                           AS "subplot_size",
        /* macroplot size (only where proportion basis = 'MACR') */
        CASE
            WHEN c."proportion_basis" = 'MACR'
                 AND p."adjustment_factor_for_the_macroplot" > 0
            THEN  p."expansion_factor"
                * c."condition_proportion_unadjusted"
                * p."adjustment_factor_for_the_macroplot"
        END                                                           AS "macroplot_size"
    FROM USFS_FIA.USFS_FIA."CONDITION"  AS c
    JOIN USFS_FIA.USFS_FIA."POPULATION" AS p
          ON  p."plot_sequence_number" = c."plot_sequence_number"
          AND p."inventory_year"       = c."inventory_year"
          AND p."state_code"           = c."state_code"
          AND p."survey_unit_code"     = c."survey_unit_code"
          AND p."county_code"          = c."county_code"
          AND p."phase_2_plot_number"  = c."phase_2_plot_number"
    WHERE p."evaluation_type"      = 'EXPCURR'
      AND c."condition_status_code" = 1          -- forest land
      AND c."inventory_year" IN (2015, 2016, 2017)
),
/*--------------------------------------------------------------------
  Compute average subplot and macroplot sizes by state & year.
--------------------------------------------------------------------*/
avg_sizes AS (
    SELECT
        "year",
        'Subplot'            AS "plot_type",
        "state_code",
        "state_name",
        AVG("subplot_size")  AS "avg_size"
    FROM base
    WHERE "subplot_size" IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY "year", "state_code", "state_name"

    UNION ALL

    SELECT
        "year",
        'Macroplot'          AS "plot_type",
        "state_code",
        "state_name",
        AVG("macroplot_size") AS "avg_size"
    FROM base
    WHERE "macroplot_size" IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY "year", "state_code", "state_name"
),
/*--------------------------------------------------------------------
  For each year and plot type, find the state with the largest average
  size.
--------------------------------------------------------------------*/
ranked AS (
    SELECT
        "plot_type",
        "year",
        "state_name",
        "avg_size",
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY "plot_type", "year"
                           ORDER BY "avg_size" DESC) AS rn
    FROM avg_sizes
)
SELECT
    "plot_type",
    "year",
    "state_name"          AS "state",
    ROUND("avg_size", 4)  AS "average_size"
FROM ranked
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY "plot_type", "year";

question: Determine which year had the earliest date after January on which more than 10 sightings of Sterna paradisaea were recorded north of 40 degrees latitude. For each year, find the first day after January with over 10 sightings of this species in that region, and identify the year whose earliest such date is the earliest among all years.
SQL:
WITH filtered AS (
    SELECT
        "year",
        "month",
        "day"
    FROM GBIF.GBIF."OCCURRENCES"
    WHERE
        -- Arctic Tern
        (
            UPPER("species") = 'STERNA PARADISAEA'
            OR UPPER("scientificname") LIKE 'STERNA PARADISAEA%'
        )
        -- north of (or on) 40° latitude
        AND "decimallatitude" >= 40
        -- after January
        AND "month" > 1
        -- ensure all date parts are present
        AND "year" IS NOT NULL
        AND "day"  IS NOT NULL
), daily_counts AS (
    SELECT
        "year",
        "month",
        "day",
        COUNT(*) AS cnt
    FROM filtered
    GROUP BY "year","month","day"
    HAVING cnt > 10             -- more than 10 sightings on that day
), first_day_per_year AS (
    SELECT
        "year",
        MIN(DATE_FROM_PARTS("year","month","day")) AS first_date
    FROM daily_counts
    GROUP BY "year"
)
SELECT "year"
FROM first_day_per_year
ORDER BY first_date, "year"
LIMIT 1;

question: Which single port, listed under region number '6585', is located within a U.S. state boundary and appears most frequently inside the geographic areas of named tropical storms with wind speeds of at least 35 knots in the North Atlantic basin, excluding those labeled 'NOT_NAMED'?
SQL:
WITH us_state_ports AS (  -- 1.  Ports in region 6585 that lie inside any U.S. state
    SELECT
        p."index_number",
        p."port_name",
        TO_GEOGRAPHY(p."port_geom")            AS port_geo
    FROM NOAA_PORTS.GEO_INTERNATIONAL_PORTS.WORLD_PORT_INDEX       p
    JOIN NOAA_PORTS.GEO_US_BOUNDARIES.STATES                       s
          ON ST_WITHIN( TO_GEOGRAPHY(p."port_geom"),
                       TO_GEOGRAPHY(s."state_geom") )
    WHERE p."region_number" = '6585'
),

storm_points AS (          -- 2.  Storm positions meeting the criteria
    SELECT
        h."sid",
        h."name",
        h."latitude",
        h."longitude",
        COALESCE(h."usa_wind", h."wmo_wind")                AS wind_kt,
        GREATEST(                                           -- maximum 34-kt radius (nm)
            COALESCE(h."usa_r34_ne",0),
            COALESCE(h."usa_r34_se",0),
            COALESCE(h."usa_r34_sw",0),
            COALESCE(h."usa_r34_nw",0)
        )                                                   AS r34_nm
    FROM NOAA_PORTS.NOAA_HURRICANES.HURRICANES  h
    WHERE h."basin"      = 'NA'
      AND h."name"      <> 'NOT_NAMED'
      AND COALESCE(h."usa_wind", h."wmo_wind", 0) >= 35     -- ≥35 kt
      AND h."latitude"  IS NOT NULL
      AND h."longitude" IS NOT NULL
),

storm_points_geo AS (      -- 3.  Add geography point, keep rows with a usable wind radius
    SELECT
        "sid",
        "name",
        TO_GEOGRAPHY( ST_MAKEPOINT("longitude", "latitude") )  AS storm_geo,
        r34_nm
    FROM storm_points
    WHERE r34_nm > 0
),

port_storm_hits AS (       -- 4.  Count how many named storms envelop each port
    SELECT
        p."port_name",
        COUNT( DISTINCT s."sid")  AS storm_cnt
    FROM us_state_ports      p
    JOIN storm_points_geo    s
      ON ST_DISTANCE( p.port_geo, s.storm_geo )    <= s.r34_nm * 1852  -- nm → metres
    GROUP BY p."port_name"
)

SELECT
    "port_name"
FROM port_storm_hits
ORDER BY storm_cnt DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Please provide a detailed comparison of the solar potential for each state, distinguishing between postal code and census tract levels. For each state, include the total number of buildings available for solar installations, the average percentage of Google Maps area covered by Project Sunroof, the average percentage of that coverage which is suitable for solar, the total potential panel count, the total kilowatt capacity, the energy generation potential, the carbon dioxide offset, the current number of buildings with solar panels, and the gap in potential installations calculated by adjusting the total qualified buildings with the coverage and suitability percentages and subtracting the current installations.
SQL:
SELECT 
    "state_name",
    'POSTAL_CODE' AS "geography_level",
    SUM("count_qualified") AS "total_qualified_buildings",
    AVG("percent_covered") AS "avg_percent_covered",
    AVG("percent_qualified") AS "avg_percent_qualified",
    SUM("number_of_panels_total") AS "total_potential_panel_count",
    SUM("kw_total") AS "total_kw_capacity",
    SUM("yearly_sunlight_kwh_total") AS "total_energy_generation_potential_kwh",
    SUM("carbon_offset_metric_tons") AS "total_carbon_offset_metric_tons",
    SUM("existing_installs_count") AS "current_solar_installations",
    SUM(("count_qualified" * ("percent_covered"/100) * ("percent_qualified"/100))) - SUM("existing_installs_count") AS "gap_in_potential_installations"
FROM SUNROOF_SOLAR.SUNROOF_SOLAR.SOLAR_POTENTIAL_BY_POSTAL_CODE
GROUP BY "state_name"

UNION ALL

SELECT 
    "state_name",
    'CENSUS_TRACT' AS "geography_level",
    SUM("count_qualified") AS "total_qualified_buildings",
    AVG("percent_covered") AS "avg_percent_covered",
    AVG("percent_qualified") AS "avg_percent_qualified",
    SUM("number_of_panels_total") AS "total_potential_panel_count",
    SUM("kw_total") AS "total_kw_capacity",
    SUM("yearly_sunlight_kwh_total") AS "total_energy_generation_potential_kwh",
    SUM("carbon_offset_metric_tons") AS "total_carbon_offset_metric_tons",
    SUM("existing_installs_count") AS "current_solar_installations",
    SUM(("count_qualified" * ("percent_covered"/100) * ("percent_qualified"/100))) - SUM("existing_installs_count") AS "gap_in_potential_installations"
FROM SUNROOF_SOLAR.SUNROOF_SOLAR.SOLAR_POTENTIAL_BY_CENSUSTRACT
GROUP BY "state_name"

question: Identify which start positions are associated with missense variants in the BRCA1 gene on chromosome 17, where the reference base is 'C' and the alternate base is 'T'. Using data from the gnomAD v2.1.1 version.
SQL:
SELECT DISTINCT "start_position"
FROM (
    /* Exome variants */
    SELECT
        e."start_position"
    FROM GNOMAD.GNOMAD."V2_1_1_EXOMES__CHR17" e,
         LATERAL FLATTEN(input => e."alternate_bases") f
    WHERE e."start_position" BETWEEN 41196312 AND 41277500      -- BRCA1 coordinates
      AND e."reference_bases" = 'C'                             -- C→T change
      AND f.value:"alt"::STRING = 'T'
      AND LOWER(f.value::STRING) LIKE '%missense%'              -- missense consequence

    UNION ALL

    /* Genome variants */
    SELECT
        g."start_position"
    FROM GNOMAD.GNOMAD."V2_1_1_GENOMES__CHR17" g,
         LATERAL FLATTEN(input => g."alternate_bases") f
    WHERE g."start_position" BETWEEN 41196312 AND 41277500
      AND g."reference_bases" = 'C'
      AND f.value:"alt"::STRING = 'T'
      AND LOWER(f.value::STRING) LIKE '%missense%'
) combined
ORDER BY "start_position";

question: Using the gnomAD v2.1.1 genomes data for chromosome 17, determine the smallest start position and largest end position of any variant whose nested VEP annotations contain the symbol 'BRCA1'. Then, for all variants whose positions fall within that gene region, retrieve the 'Protein_position' values only if the 'Consequence' includes 'missense_variant', sort them in ascending order by 'Protein_position', and finally output the first such result.
SQL:
WITH brca1_bounds AS (  -- 1. determine genomic span of BRCA1 on chr17 (v2.1.1 genomes)
    SELECT
        MIN(t."start_position") AS min_start,
        MAX(t."end_position")   AS max_end
    FROM GNOMAD.GNOMAD."V2_1_1_GENOMES__CHR17"   t
         ,LATERAL FLATTEN(input => t."alternate_bases") ab
         ,LATERAL FLATTEN(input => ab.value:"vep")       v
    WHERE t."start_position" BETWEEN 40000000 AND 42000000   -- BRCA1 region (GRCh37 ≈41 Mb)
      AND v.value:"SYMBOL"::STRING = 'BRCA1'
),
missense_pp AS (        -- 2. collect Protein_position of missense variants within that span
    SELECT DISTINCT
           v2.value:"Protein_position"::STRING                           AS protein_position,
           TRY_TO_NUMBER(SPLIT_PART(v2.value:"Protein_position"::STRING, '/', 1)) AS pos_num
    FROM GNOMAD.GNOMAD."V2_1_1_GENOMES__CHR17"  t
         JOIN brca1_bounds b
           ON t."start_position" BETWEEN b.min_start AND b.max_end
         ,LATERAL FLATTEN(input => t."alternate_bases") ab2
         ,LATERAL FLATTEN(input => ab2.value:"vep")       v2
    WHERE v2.value:"Consequence"::STRING ILIKE '%missense_variant%'
      AND v2.value:"Protein_position" IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT protein_position
FROM missense_pp
ORDER BY pos_num
LIMIT 1;

question: Generate summary statistics on genetic variants in the region between positions 55039447 and 55064852 on chromosome 1. This includes the number of variants, the total allele count, the total number of alleles, and distinct gene symbols (using Variant Effect Predictor, VEP, for gene annotation). Additionally, compute the density of mutations by dividing the length of the region by the number of variants.  Using data from the gnomAD v3 version.
SQL:
/*  Summary statistics for variants in chr1:55,039,447-55,064,852 (gnomAD v3 genomes)  */
WITH region_variants AS (
    SELECT *
    FROM GNOMAD.GNOMAD."V3_GENOMES__CHR1"
    WHERE "reference_name" = 'chr1'
      AND "start_position" BETWEEN 55039447 AND 55064852
),
/* basic counts per‐variant (row) */
variant_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*)                              AS variant_count,
        SUM("AN")                             AS total_alleles           -- total AN across variants
    FROM region_variants
),
/* sum AC across every alternate allele (must FLATTEN alternate_bases) */
ac_totals AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM((ab.value:"AC")::NUMBER)          AS total_allele_count      -- genome-wide AC within region
    FROM region_variants, 
         LATERAL FLATTEN(input => region_variants."alternate_bases") ab
),
/* collect distinct gene symbols from VEP annotations */
gene_symbols AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT 
           vep.value:"SYMBOL"::STRING         AS gene_symbol
    FROM region_variants,
         LATERAL FLATTEN(input => region_variants."alternate_bases") ab,
         LATERAL FLATTEN(input => ab.value:"vep") vep
    WHERE vep.value:"SYMBOL" IS NOT NULL 
          AND vep.value:"SYMBOL" <> ''
),
gene_list AS (
    SELECT 
        LISTAGG(gene_symbol, ',') 
            WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY gene_symbol) AS gene_symbols
    FROM gene_symbols
)
/* final summary */
SELECT
    vs.variant_count,
    vs.total_alleles,
    ac.total_allele_count,
    gl.gene_symbols,
    (55064852 - 55039447 + 1)::FLOAT / vs.variant_count AS mutation_density_bp_per_variant
FROM variant_stats      AS vs
CROSS JOIN ac_totals    AS ac
CROSS JOIN gene_list    AS gl;

question: Based on the most recent refresh date, identify the top-ranked rising search term for the week that is exactly one year prior to the latest available week in the dataset.
SQL:
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    tr."term",
    tr."week",
    tr."score",
    tr."rank",
    tr."percent_gain",
    tr."refresh_date"
  FROM GOOGLE_TRENDS.GOOGLE_TRENDS.TOP_RISING_TERMS tr
  WHERE tr."week" IS NOT NULL

  UNION ALL

  SELECT
    ir."term",
    ir."week",
    ir."score",
    ir."rank",
    ir."percent_gain",
    ir."refresh_date"
  FROM GOOGLE_TRENDS.GOOGLE_TRENDS.INTERNATIONAL_TOP_RISING_TERMS ir
  WHERE ir."week" IS NOT NULL
),
latest_refresh AS (
  SELECT MAX("refresh_date") AS "refresh_date"
  FROM base
),
latest_week_in_latest AS (
  SELECT MAX(b."week") AS "week"
  FROM base b
  JOIN latest_refresh lr ON b."refresh_date" = lr."refresh_date"
),
target AS (
  -- Use 52 weeks prior to align with weekly cadence for "exactly one year"
  SELECT DATEADD('week', -52, lw."week") AS "target_week"
  FROM latest_week_in_latest lw
),
cand_exact_latest AS (  -- priority 1: exact target week within latest refresh
  SELECT 1 AS prio, b."refresh_date", b."week"
  FROM base b
  JOIN latest_refresh lr ON b."refresh_date" = lr."refresh_date"
  JOIN target t ON b."week" = t."target_week"
  GROUP BY b."refresh_date", b."week"
),
cand_exact_any AS (     -- priority 2: exact target week across any refresh (choose latest refresh that has it)
  SELECT 2 AS prio, x."refresh_date", x."week"
  FROM (
    SELECT b."refresh_date", b."week",
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY b."week" ORDER BY b."refresh_date" DESC) AS rn
    FROM base b
    JOIN target t ON b."week" = t."target_week"
  ) x
  WHERE x.rn = 1
),
candidates AS (
  SELECT * FROM cand_exact_latest
  UNION ALL
  SELECT * FROM cand_exact_any
),
selected_context AS (
  SELECT "refresh_date", "week"
  FROM (
    SELECT "refresh_date", "week",
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY prio) AS rn
    FROM candidates
  )
  WHERE rn = 1
),
rows_in_context AS (
  SELECT b.*
  FROM base b
  JOIN selected_context sc
    ON b."refresh_date" = sc."refresh_date"
   AND b."week" = sc."week"
),
terms_aggregated AS (
  SELECT
    "term",
    MIN("rank") AS best_rank,
    MAX("percent_gain") AS best_percent_gain,
    MAX("score") AS best_score
  FROM rows_in_context
  GROUP BY "term"
)
SELECT ta."term"
FROM terms_aggregated ta
QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
  ORDER BY ta.best_rank ASC NULLS LAST,
           ta.best_percent_gain DESC NULLS LAST,
           ta.best_score DESC NULLS LAST,
           ta."term"
) = 1;

question: Within the 2015 dataset for accidents that occurred from January through August and involved more than one distinct person, what percentage of these accidents had more than one individual with a severe injury (injury severity = 4)
SQL:
WITH accident_data AS (
    SELECT 
        "consecutive_number",
        COUNT(DISTINCT "person_number") AS person_count,
        SUM(CASE WHEN "injury_severity" = 4 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS severe_injury_count
    FROM 
        NHTSA_TRAFFIC_FATALITIES.NHTSA_TRAFFIC_FATALITIES.PERSON_2015
    WHERE 
        "month_of_crash" BETWEEN 1 AND 8
    GROUP BY 
        "consecutive_number"
)
SELECT 
    (COUNT(CASE WHEN severe_injury_count > 1 THEN 1 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) * 100 AS percentage
FROM 
    accident_data
WHERE 
    person_count > 1;

question: Which top 3 states had the largest differences in the number of traffic accidents between rainy and clear weather during weekends in 2016? Please also provide the respective differences for each state.
SQL:
SELECT
    "state_name",
    SUM(CASE WHEN "atmospheric_conditions_name" = 'Rain'  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS rain_accidents,
    SUM(CASE WHEN "atmospheric_conditions_name" = 'Clear' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS clear_accidents,
    ABS(
        SUM(CASE WHEN "atmospheric_conditions_name" = 'Rain'  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) -
        SUM(CASE WHEN "atmospheric_conditions_name" = 'Clear' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
    ) AS difference_in_accidents
FROM
    NHTSA_TRAFFIC_FATALITIES.NHTSA_TRAFFIC_FATALITIES.ACCIDENT_2016
WHERE
    "year_of_crash" = 2016
    AND "day_of_week" IN (1, 7)                       -- 1 = Sunday, 7 = Saturday (weekends)
    AND "atmospheric_conditions_name" IN ('Rain', 'Clear')
GROUP BY
    "state_name"
ORDER BY
    difference_in_accidents DESC
LIMIT 3;

question: What is the increasing amount of the average earnings per job between the years 2012 and 2017 for each geographic region in Massachusetts (indicated by "MA" at the end of GeoName)?
SQL:
SELECT 
    t2017."GeoName",
    t2017."Earnings_per_job_avg" - t2012."Earnings_per_job_avg" AS "Earnings_Increase"
FROM 
    (SELECT * 
     FROM SDOH.SDOH_BEA_CAINC30.FIPS
     WHERE YEAR("Year") = 2017 AND "GeoName" LIKE '%MA') AS t2017
JOIN 
    (SELECT * 
     FROM SDOH.SDOH_BEA_CAINC30.FIPS
     WHERE YEAR("Year") = 2012 AND "GeoName" LIKE '%MA') AS t2012
ON t2017."GeoName" = t2012."GeoName"
ORDER BY t2017."GeoName";

question: Identify the top 10 regions (counties) with the highest total number of SNAP-participating households, using the 2017 5-year ACS county-level data and SNAP enrollment data from January 1, 2017, excluding regions where the total SNAP participation is zero. For each of these regions, calculate the ratio of households earning under $20,000 to the total number of SNAP-participating households.
SQL:
WITH snap_households AS (
    SELECT
        "FIPS"                              AS "geo_id",
        SUM("SNAP_All_Participation_Households") AS "total_snap_households"
    FROM SDOH.SDOH_SNAP_ENROLLMENT.SNAP_ENROLLMENT
    WHERE "Date" = '2017-01-01'
    GROUP BY "FIPS"
),
acs_income AS (
    SELECT
        "geo_id",
        COALESCE("income_less_10000", 0)
        + COALESCE("income_10000_14999", 0)
        + COALESCE("income_15000_19999", 0)    AS "households_under_20000"
    FROM SDOH.CENSUS_BUREAU_ACS.COUNTY_2017_5YR
)
SELECT
    a."geo_id"                                            AS "county_fips",
    s."total_snap_households",
    a."households_under_20000",
    ROUND(
        a."households_under_20000" / s."total_snap_households",
        4
    )                                                     AS "under_20k_to_snap_ratio"
FROM snap_households s
JOIN acs_income a
  ON a."geo_id" = s."geo_id"
WHERE s."total_snap_households" > 0
ORDER BY s."total_snap_households" DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: What is the change in the number of homeless veterans between 2012 and 2018 for each CoC region in New York that has data available in both years?
SQL:
SELECT 
    ny_2012."CoC_Number", 
    ny_2012."CoC_Name", 
    COALESCE(ny_2018."Homeless_Veterans", 0) AS "Homeless_Veterans_2018", 
    COALESCE(ny_2012."Homeless_Veterans", 0) AS "Homeless_Veterans_2012",
    COALESCE(ny_2018."Homeless_Veterans", 0) - COALESCE(ny_2012."Homeless_Veterans", 0) AS "Change_In_Homeless_Veterans"
FROM 
    SDOH.SDOH_HUD_PIT_HOMELESSNESS.HUD_PIT_BY_COC ny_2012
JOIN 
    SDOH.SDOH_HUD_PIT_HOMELESSNESS.HUD_PIT_BY_COC ny_2018 
ON 
    ny_2012."CoC_Number" = ny_2018."CoC_Number"
WHERE 
    ny_2012."Count_Year" = 2012 AND 
    ny_2018."Count_Year" = 2018 AND 
    ny_2012."CoC_Number" LIKE 'NY-%';

question: Calculate the percentage change in the total number of unsheltered homeless people from 2015 to 2018 for each state by summing the counts over all Continuums of Care (CoCs) within each state. Then, determine the national average of these state percentage changes. Identify the five states whose percentage change is closest to this national average percentage change. Please provide the state abbreviations.
SQL:
WITH state_year AS (
    SELECT
        SPLIT_PART("CoC_Number", '-', 1)                AS "State",
        "Count_Year"                                    AS "Year",
        SUM("Unsheltered_Homeless")                     AS "Unsheltered_Total"
    FROM SDOH.SDOH_HUD_PIT_HOMELESSNESS.HUD_PIT_BY_COC
    WHERE "Count_Year" IN (2015, 2018)
    GROUP BY
        SPLIT_PART("CoC_Number", '-', 1),
        "Count_Year"
),
state_change AS (
    SELECT
        s2015."State",
        s2015."Unsheltered_Total"                       AS "Unsheltered_2015",
        s2018."Unsheltered_Total"                       AS "Unsheltered_2018",
        CASE 
            WHEN s2015."Unsheltered_Total" = 0 THEN NULL
            ELSE ( (s2018."Unsheltered_Total" - s2015."Unsheltered_Total") 
                   / s2015."Unsheltered_Total" ) * 100
        END                                             AS "Pct_Change"
    FROM state_year s2015
    JOIN state_year s2018
          ON s2015."State" = s2018."State"
         AND s2015."Year"  = 2015
         AND s2018."Year"  = 2018
),
national_avg AS (
    SELECT AVG("Pct_Change") AS "Avg_Pct_Change"
    FROM state_change
    WHERE "Pct_Change" IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    sc."State"
FROM state_change sc
JOIN national_avg na
  ON 1=1
WHERE sc."Pct_Change" IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY ABS(sc."Pct_Change" - na."Avg_Pct_Change") ASC
LIMIT 5;

question: Please list the average number of prenatal weeks in 2018 for counties in Wisconsin where more than 5% of the employed population had commutes of 45-59 minutes in 2017.
SQL:
WITH Wisconsin_Commute_2017 AS (
    SELECT
        SUBSTRING("geo_id", 1, 5) AS "County_FIPS",
        SUM("commute_45_59_mins") AS "Commute_45_59_Mins",
        SUM("employed_pop") AS "Employed_Pop"
    FROM SDOH.CENSUS_BUREAU_ACS.BLOCKGROUP_2017_5YR
    WHERE SUBSTRING("geo_id", 1, 2) = '55'  -- Wisconsin FIPS Code
    GROUP BY SUBSTRING("geo_id", 1, 5)
    HAVING SUM("employed_pop") > 0  -- Avoid division by zero
    AND (SUM("commute_45_59_mins") / SUM("employed_pop")) > 0.05
),
Wisconsin_Natality_2018 AS (
    SELECT
        "County_of_Residence_FIPS",
        AVG("Ave_Number_of_Prenatal_Wks") AS "Average_Prenatal_Wks"
    FROM SDOH.SDOH_CDC_WONDER_NATALITY.COUNTY_NATALITY
    WHERE "Year" = '2018-01-01'
    AND "County_of_Residence" LIKE '%, WI'
    GROUP BY "County_of_Residence_FIPS"
)
SELECT DISTINCT 
    WN."County_of_Residence_FIPS",
    WN."Average_Prenatal_Wks"
FROM Wisconsin_Commute_2017 WC
JOIN Wisconsin_Natality_2018 WN
ON WC."County_FIPS" = WN."County_of_Residence_FIPS";

question: Count the number of counties that experienced an increase in unemployment from 2015 to 2018, using 5-year ACS data, and a decrease in dual-eligible enrollee counts between December 1, 2015, and December 1, 2018.
SQL:
WITH
-- 1.  Unemployment figures for 2015 and 2018
unemployment AS (
    SELECT
        a."geo_id"                                    AS "geo_id",
        a."unemployed_pop"                            AS "unemployed_2015",
        b."unemployed_pop"                            AS "unemployed_2018"
    FROM  SDOH.CENSUS_BUREAU_ACS."COUNTY_2015_5YR" a
    INNER JOIN SDOH.CENSUS_BUREAU_ACS."COUNTY_2018_5YR" b
            ON a."geo_id" = b."geo_id"
),
-- 2.  Counties where unemployment rose from 2015 → 2018
unemployment_increase AS (
    SELECT
        "geo_id"
    FROM   unemployment
    WHERE  "unemployed_2018" > "unemployed_2015"
),
-- 3.  Dual-eligible enrollment on 2015-12-01 and 2018-12-01
dual_enrollment AS (
    SELECT
        "FIPS"                                                         AS "geo_id",
        SUM(CASE WHEN "Date" = DATE '2015-12-01' THEN "Public_Total" END) AS "dual_2015",
        SUM(CASE WHEN "Date" = DATE '2018-12-01' THEN "Public_Total" END) AS "dual_2018"
    FROM   SDOH.SDOH_CMS_DUAL_ELIGIBLE_ENROLLMENT."DUAL_ELIGIBLE_ENROLLMENT_BY_COUNTY_AND_PROGRAM"
    WHERE  "Date" IN (DATE '2015-12-01', DATE '2018-12-01')
    GROUP  BY "FIPS"
),
-- 4.  Counties where dual-eligible enrollment fell from 2015 → 2018
dual_decrease AS (
    SELECT
        "geo_id"
    FROM   dual_enrollment
    WHERE  "dual_2015" IS NOT NULL
       AND "dual_2018" IS NOT NULL
       AND "dual_2018" < "dual_2015"
)
-- 5.  Count of counties meeting BOTH criteria
SELECT
    COUNT(*) AS "count_counties_with_unemp_increase_and_dual_decrease"
FROM
    unemployment_increase ui
    INNER JOIN dual_decrease dd
        ON ui."geo_id" = dd."geo_id";

question: Could you assess the relationship between the poverty rates from the previous year's census data and the percentage of births without maternal morbidity for the years 2016 to 2018? Use only data for births where no maternal morbidity was reported and for each year, use the 5-year census data from the year before to compute the Pearson correlation coefficient
SQL:
WITH natality AS (
    /* 1.  Births per county & year, split by maternal morbidity status */
    SELECT
        YEAR("Year")                         AS birth_year,
        "County_of_Residence_FIPS"           AS county_fips_txt,
        TO_NUMBER("County_of_Residence_FIPS") AS county_fips_num,
        SUM("Births")                        AS total_births,
        /* births where NO maternal morbidity was reported */
        SUM(CASE WHEN "Maternal_Morbidity_Desc" = 'None checked' THEN "Births" ELSE 0 END) 
                                              AS births_no_morbidity
    FROM SDOH.SDOH_CDC_WONDER_NATALITY.COUNTY_NATALITY_BY_MATERNAL_MORBIDITY
    WHERE YEAR("Year") BETWEEN 2016 AND 2018
    GROUP BY YEAR("Year"), "County_of_Residence_FIPS"
), natality_pct AS (
    /* 2.  Percentage of births without maternal morbidity */
    SELECT
        birth_year,
        county_fips_num                      AS county_fips_num,
        100.0 * births_no_morbidity / NULLIF(total_births,0) 
                                              AS pct_no_morbidity
    FROM natality
    WHERE total_births > 0
), census AS (
    /* 3.  Poverty rate per county for each census 5-year table */
    SELECT 2015 AS census_year,
           TO_NUMBER("geo_id")               AS county_fips_num,
           100.0 * "poverty" / NULLIF("pop_determined_poverty_status",0)
                                             AS poverty_rate
    FROM SDOH.CENSUS_BUREAU_ACS.COUNTY_2015_5YR
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 2016,
           TO_NUMBER("geo_id"),
           100.0 * "poverty" / NULLIF("pop_determined_poverty_status",0)
    FROM SDOH.CENSUS_BUREAU_ACS.COUNTY_2016_5YR
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 2017,
           TO_NUMBER("geo_id"),
           100.0 * "poverty" / NULLIF("pop_determined_poverty_status",0)
    FROM SDOH.CENSUS_BUREAU_ACS.COUNTY_2017_5YR
), combined AS (
    /* 4.  Match each birth year with poverty data from the previous census year */
    SELECT
        n.birth_year,
        n.pct_no_morbidity,
        c.poverty_rate
    FROM natality_pct n
    JOIN census c
      ON c.county_fips_num = n.county_fips_num
     AND c.census_year     = n.birth_year - 1
    WHERE c.poverty_rate IS NOT NULL
      AND n.pct_no_morbidity IS NOT NULL
)
/* 5.  Pearson correlation per year between county-level poverty rate
       (previous year's census) and % births without maternal morbidity */
SELECT
    birth_year                                                    AS "YEAR",
    CORR(poverty_rate, pct_no_morbidity)                          AS "PEARSON_CORR_POVERTY_vs_NO_MATERNAL_MORBIDITY_PCT"
FROM combined
GROUP BY birth_year
ORDER BY birth_year;

question: Which three cities have the largest difference between their 1990 EPA PM2.5 measurements (using units_of_measure = 'Micrograms/cubic meter (LC)' and parameter_name = 'Acceptable PM2.5 AQI & Speciation Mass') and their 2020 OpenAQ PM2.5 measurements (where pollutant = 'pm25' based on the year extracted from the timestamp), with both datasets matched by latitude and longitude rounded to two decimals, and the difference ordered from greatest to least?
SQL:
WITH epa_1990 AS (
    -- 1990 EPA PM2.5 (Acceptable PM2.5 AQI & Speciation Mass)
    SELECT
        ROUND("latitude", 2)  AS lat2,
        ROUND("longitude", 2) AS lon2,
        MIN("city_name")      AS city,
        AVG("arithmetic_mean") AS epa_pm25_1990
    FROM OPENAQ.EPA_HISTORICAL_AIR_QUALITY.PM25_NONFRM_DAILY_SUMMARY
    WHERE "parameter_name"   = 'Acceptable PM2.5 AQI & Speciation Mass'
      AND "units_of_measure" = 'Micrograms/cubic meter (LC)'
      AND YEAR("date_local") = 1990
    GROUP BY
        ROUND("latitude", 2),
        ROUND("longitude", 2)
),
openaq_2020 AS (
    -- 2020 OpenAQ PM2.5 (pollutant = 'pm25')
    SELECT
        ROUND("latitude", 2)  AS lat2,
        ROUND("longitude", 2) AS lon2,
        MIN("city")           AS city,
        AVG("value")          AS openaq_pm25_2020
    FROM OPENAQ.OPENAQ.GLOBAL_AIR_QUALITY
    WHERE "pollutant" = 'pm25'
      AND YEAR(TO_TIMESTAMP_NTZ("timestamp"/1000000)) = 2020
    GROUP BY
        ROUND("latitude", 2),
        ROUND("longitude", 2)
),
paired AS (
    -- match by rounded lat/lon
    SELECT
        o.city,
        o.openaq_pm25_2020,
        e.epa_pm25_1990,
        ABS(o.openaq_pm25_2020 - e.epa_pm25_1990) AS diff
    FROM openaq_2020 o
    JOIN epa_1990   e
      ON o.lat2 = e.lat2
     AND o.lon2 = e.lon2
)
SELECT
    city,
    ROUND(diff, 4) AS difference_micrograms_per_cubic_meter
FROM paired
ORDER BY diff DESC
LIMIT 3;

question: Which U.S. state reported the highest total annual revenue in billions of dollars during fiscal year 2016, considering companies that provided four quarters of data and reported measure tags in ('Revenues','SalesRevenueNet','SalesRevenueGoodsNet'), excluding any entries where the state field (stprba) is null or empty?
SQL:
WITH CTE_Revenue AS (
    SELECT 
        "SUBMISSION"."stprba" AS "State", 
        SUM("QUICK_SUMMARY"."value") / 1000000000 AS "Total_Revenue_Billion"
    FROM 
        "SEC_QUARTERLY_FINANCIALS"."SEC_QUARTERLY_FINANCIALS"."QUICK_SUMMARY"
    INNER JOIN 
        "SEC_QUARTERLY_FINANCIALS"."SEC_QUARTERLY_FINANCIALS"."SUBMISSION"
        ON "QUICK_SUMMARY"."submission_number" = "SUBMISSION"."submission_number"
    WHERE 
        "QUICK_SUMMARY"."fiscal_year" = 2016
        AND "QUICK_SUMMARY"."number_of_quarters" = 4
        AND "QUICK_SUMMARY"."measure_tag" IN ('Revenues', 'SalesRevenueNet', 'SalesRevenueGoodsNet')
        AND "SUBMISSION"."stprba" IS NOT NULL
        AND "SUBMISSION"."stprba" <> ''
    GROUP BY 
        "SUBMISSION"."stprba"
)
SELECT 
    "State", 
    "Total_Revenue_Billion"
FROM 
    CTE_Revenue
ORDER BY 
    "Total_Revenue_Billion" DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Identify and rank the top 10 tags from Stack Overflow questions that were referenced in Hacker News comments on or after 2014 by counting how many times each question was mentioned, then splitting the questions’ tag strings by the '|' delimiter, grouping by tag
SQL:
WITH filtered_comments AS (
    SELECT 
        "text"
    FROM 
        "STACKOVERFLOW_PLUS"."HACKERNEWS"."COMMENTSV2"
    WHERE 
        "time" >= 1388534400  -- Timestamp for 2014-01-01
),
extracted_question_ids AS (
    SELECT 
        REGEXP_SUBSTR("text", 'stackoverflow.com/questions/([0-9]+)', 1, 1, 'e', 1) AS "question_id"
    FROM 
        filtered_comments
    WHERE 
        "text" ILIKE '%stackoverflow.com/questions/%'
),
question_tags AS (
    SELECT 
        "tags"
    FROM 
        extracted_question_ids e
    JOIN 
        "STACKOVERFLOW_PLUS"."STACKOVERFLOW"."POSTS_QUESTIONS" q
    ON 
        CAST(e."question_id" AS NUMBER) = q."id"
),
split_tags AS (
    SELECT
        TRIM("tag_table"."VALUE") AS "tag"
    FROM 
        question_tags,
        LATERAL SPLIT_TO_TABLE("tags", '|') AS "tag_table"
)
SELECT 
    "tag", 
    COUNT(*) AS "frequency"
FROM 
    split_tags
GROUP BY 
    "tag"
ORDER BY 
    "frequency" DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: Compute the monthly statistics for new StackOverflow users created in 2021. For each month, report the total number of new users, the percentage of these new users who asked at least one question within 30 days of signing up, and among those who asked a question within 30 days, the percentage who then answered at least one question after their first question and within 30 days following their first question.
SQL:
WITH Users2021 AS (
    SELECT "id", 
           "creation_date",
           TO_CHAR(TO_TIMESTAMP("creation_date" / 1e6), 'YYYY-MM') AS "creation_month"
    FROM STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.USERS
    WHERE "creation_date" >= 1609459200000000 AND "creation_date" < 1640995200000000
),
FirstQuestion AS (
    SELECT "owner_user_id", MIN("creation_date") AS "first_question_date"
    FROM STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.POSTS_QUESTIONS
    GROUP BY "owner_user_id"
),
Questions30Days AS (
    SELECT fq."owner_user_id"
    FROM FirstQuestion fq
    JOIN Users2021 u ON fq."owner_user_id" = u."id"
    WHERE fq."first_question_date" <= u."creation_date" + 30 * 24 * 3600 * 1e6
),
AnswersAfterQuestion AS (
    SELECT pa."owner_user_id"
    FROM STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.POSTS_ANSWERS pa
    JOIN Questions30Days qd ON pa."owner_user_id" = qd."owner_user_id"
    JOIN FirstQuestion fq ON pa."owner_user_id" = fq."owner_user_id"
    WHERE pa."creation_date" > fq."first_question_date"
      AND pa."creation_date" <= fq."first_question_date" + 30 * 24 * 3600 * 1e6
    GROUP BY pa."owner_user_id"
)
SELECT u."creation_month",
       COUNT(DISTINCT u."id") AS total_users,
       COUNT(DISTINCT qd."owner_user_id") * 100.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT u."id") AS percent_asked_question_30_days,
       COUNT(DISTINCT aq."owner_user_id") * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT qd."owner_user_id"), 0) AS percent_answered_after_question_30_days
FROM Users2021 u
LEFT JOIN Questions30Days qd ON u."id" = qd."owner_user_id"
LEFT JOIN AnswersAfterQuestion aq ON qd."owner_user_id" = aq."owner_user_id"
GROUP BY u."creation_month"
ORDER BY u."creation_month";

question: How do the average reputation and number of badges vary among Stack Overflow users based on the number of complete years they have been members, considering only those who joined on or before October 1, 2021?
SQL:
WITH
  ref AS (
    SELECT DATE '2021-10-01' AS "ref_date"
  ),
  users_base AS (
    SELECT
      "id" AS "user_id",
      CAST(TO_TIMESTAMP("creation_date" / 1000000.0) AS DATE) AS "creation_date_dt",
      "reputation"
    FROM "STACKOVERFLOW"."STACKOVERFLOW"."USERS"
  ),
  users_filtered AS (
    SELECT ub.*
    FROM users_base ub
    JOIN ref r ON 1=1
    WHERE ub."creation_date_dt" <= r."ref_date"
  ),
  users_years AS (
    SELECT
      "user_id",
      "creation_date_dt",
      "reputation",
      (DATEDIFF(year, "creation_date_dt", r."ref_date")
        - CASE WHEN DATEADD(year, DATEDIFF(year, "creation_date_dt", r."ref_date"), "creation_date_dt") > r."ref_date" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
      ) AS "complete_years"
    FROM users_filtered uf
    JOIN ref r ON 1=1
  ),
  badges_per_user AS (
    SELECT
      "user_id",
      COUNT(*) AS "num_badges"
    FROM "STACKOVERFLOW"."STACKOVERFLOW"."BADGES"
    GROUP BY "user_id"
  )
SELECT
  uy."complete_years" AS "complete_years",
  COALESCE(CAST(AVG(uy."reputation") AS FLOAT), 0) AS "avg_reputation",
  COALESCE(CAST(AVG(COALESCE(b."num_badges", 0)) AS FLOAT), 0) AS "avg_badges",
  COUNT(*) AS "user_count"
FROM users_years uy
LEFT JOIN badges_per_user b
  ON uy."user_id" = b."user_id"
GROUP BY uy."complete_years"
ORDER BY uy."complete_years" ASC;

question: You need to determine which day of the week has the third highest percentage of questions on Stack Overflow that receive an answer within an hour. To do this, use the question creation date from the posts_questions table and the earliest answer creation date from the posts_answers table. Once you’ve calculated the percentage of questions that get answered within an hour for each day, identify the day with the third highest percentage and report that percentage.
SQL:
WITH
-- all questions with their creation timestamp and day of week
QUESTIONS AS (
    SELECT
        q."id"                                 AS question_id,
        q."creation_date"                      AS q_create_us,
        TO_TIMESTAMP(q."creation_date" / 1000000) AS q_ts,
        DAYNAME(TO_TIMESTAMP(q."creation_date" / 1000000)) AS dow
    FROM STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW."POSTS_QUESTIONS" q
),
-- earliest answer time for every question that received an answer
FIRST_ANSWERS AS (
    SELECT
        a."parent_id"                          AS question_id,
        MIN(a."creation_date")                 AS first_answer_us
    FROM STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW."POSTS_ANSWERS" a
    WHERE a."parent_id" IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY a."parent_id"
),
-- join questions with their earliest answer (if any) and flag if answered within 1 hour
JOINED AS (
    SELECT
        q.dow,
        CASE
            WHEN fa.first_answer_us IS NOT NULL
                 AND fa.first_answer_us - q.q_create_us <= 3600 * 1000000 -- 1 hour in micro-seconds
            THEN 1 ELSE 0
        END AS answered_within_hour
    FROM QUESTIONS q
    LEFT JOIN FIRST_ANSWERS fa
           ON q.question_id = fa.question_id
),
-- percentage of questions answered within an hour for each day of week
DAY_STATS AS (
    SELECT
        dow,
        ROUND(SUM(answered_within_hour) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 4) AS pct_within_hour
    FROM JOINED
    GROUP BY dow
)
-- pick the day with the 3rd-highest percentage
SELECT
    dow                       AS day_of_week,
    pct_within_hour           AS percentage_answered_within_hour
FROM DAY_STATS
QUALIFY RANK() OVER (ORDER BY pct_within_hour DESC) = 3;

question: Please provide the display name of the user who has answered the most questions on Stack Overflow, considering only users with a reputation greater than 10.
SQL:
SELECT "U"."display_name"
FROM "STACKOVERFLOW"."STACKOVERFLOW"."USERS" AS "U"
JOIN "STACKOVERFLOW"."STACKOVERFLOW"."POSTS_ANSWERS" AS "A" ON "U"."id" = "A"."owner_user_id"
WHERE "U"."reputation" > 10
GROUP BY "U"."display_name"
ORDER BY COUNT("A"."id") DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the highest number of answers received for a single Python 2 specific question on Stack Overflow, excluding any discussions that involve Python 3?
SQL:
SELECT MAX("answer_count") AS "max_python2_answers"
FROM STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.POSTS_QUESTIONS
WHERE 
    (
        LOWER("tags") LIKE '%python2%' 
        OR LOWER("tags") LIKE '%python-2%' 
        OR LOWER("tags") LIKE '%python-2.%' 
        OR LOWER("tags") LIKE '%python-2x%' 
        OR LOWER("tags") LIKE '%python2x%' 
    )
    AND (
        "tags" IS NOT NULL
        AND LOWER("tags") NOT LIKE '%python3%' 
        AND LOWER("tags") NOT LIKE '%python-3%' 
        AND LOWER("tags") NOT LIKE '%python3x%' 
        AND LOWER("tags") NOT LIKE '%python-3x%' 
        AND LOWER("tags") NOT LIKE '%python-3.%'
    )

question: Retrieve details of accepted answers to Stack Overflow questions posted in January 2016 that have tags including "javascript" and at least one of "xss", "cross-site", "exploit", or "cybersecurity"; the answers themselves must also have been posted in January 2016. For each accepted answer, include the answer's ID, the answerer's reputation, score, and comment count, along with the associated question's tags, score, answer count, the asker's reputation, view count, and comment count.
SQL:
SELECT 
    ans."id" AS answer_id,
    ans_user."reputation" AS answerer_reputation,
    ans."score" AS answer_score,
    ans."comment_count" AS answer_comment_count,
    ques."tags" AS question_tags,
    ques."score" AS question_score,
    ques."answer_count" AS question_answer_count,
    ques_user."reputation" AS asker_reputation,
    ques."view_count" AS question_view_count,
    ques."comment_count" AS question_comment_count
FROM STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.POSTS_QUESTIONS AS ques
JOIN STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.POSTS_ANSWERS AS ans
    ON ques."accepted_answer_id" = ans."id"
JOIN STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.USERS AS ques_user
    ON ques."owner_user_id" = ques_user."id"
JOIN STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.USERS AS ans_user
    ON ans."owner_user_id" = ans_user."id"
WHERE ques."creation_date" BETWEEN 1451606400000000 AND 1454284799000000
  AND ans."creation_date" BETWEEN 1451606400000000 AND 1454284799000000
  AND LOWER(ques."tags") LIKE '%javascript%'
  AND (
      LOWER(ques."tags") LIKE '%xss%' 
      OR LOWER(ques."tags") LIKE '%cross-site%'
      OR LOWER(ques."tags") LIKE '%exploit%'
      OR LOWER(ques."tags") LIKE '%cybersecurity%'
  );

question: What is the monthly proportion of Stack Overflow questions tagged with 'python' in the year 2022?
SQL:
WITH TotalQuestions AS (
    SELECT 
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_TIMESTAMP("creation_date" / 1000000)) AS "month",
        COUNT(*) AS "total_questions"
    FROM STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.POSTS_QUESTIONS
    WHERE YEAR(TO_TIMESTAMP("creation_date" / 1000000)) = 2022
    GROUP BY 1
),
PythonTaggedQuestions AS (
    SELECT 
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_TIMESTAMP("creation_date" / 1000000)) AS "month",
        COUNT(*) AS "python_questions"
    FROM STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.POSTS_QUESTIONS
    WHERE 
        YEAR(TO_TIMESTAMP("creation_date" / 1000000)) = 2022
        AND SPLIT_PART("tags", '|', 1) = 'python'  -- Adjust split logic based on actual format
    GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT 
    TQ."month",
    PTQ."python_questions" / TQ."total_questions" AS "python_proportion"
FROM 
    TotalQuestions TQ
JOIN 
    PythonTaggedQuestions PTQ
ON 
    TQ."month" = PTQ."month"
ORDER BY 
    TQ."month";

question: From July 1, 2019 through December 31, 2019, for all users with IDs between 16712208 and 18712208 on Stack Overflow, retrieve the user ID and the tags of the relevant question for each of their contributions, including comments on both questions and answers, any answers they posted, and any questions they authored, making sure to correctly associate the comment or answer with its parent question’s tags.
SQL:
WITH UserIDs AS (
    SELECT "id"
    FROM STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.USERS
    WHERE "id" BETWEEN 16712208 AND 18712208
),
Comment_Questions AS (
    SELECT C."user_id", Q."tags"
    FROM STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.COMMENTS C
    JOIN STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.POSTS_QUESTIONS Q
    ON C."post_id" = Q."id"
    WHERE C."creation_date" BETWEEN 1561939200000000 AND 1577750400000000
),
Comment_Answers AS (
    SELECT C."user_id", Q."tags"
    FROM STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.COMMENTS C
    JOIN STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.POSTS_ANSWERS A
    ON C."post_id" = A."id"
    JOIN STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.POSTS_QUESTIONS Q
    ON A."parent_id" = Q."id"
    WHERE C."creation_date" BETWEEN 1561939200000000 AND 1577750400000000
),
Answers AS (
    SELECT A."owner_user_id", Q."tags"
    FROM STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.POSTS_ANSWERS A
    JOIN STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.POSTS_QUESTIONS Q
    ON A."parent_id" = Q."id"
    WHERE A."creation_date" BETWEEN 1561939200000000 AND 1577750400000000
),
Questions AS (
    SELECT Q."owner_user_id", Q."tags"
    FROM STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.POSTS_QUESTIONS Q
    WHERE Q."creation_date" BETWEEN 1561939200000000 AND 1577750400000000
)
SELECT U."user_id", U."tags"
FROM (
    SELECT "user_id", "tags" FROM Comment_Questions
    UNION ALL
    SELECT "user_id", "tags" FROM Comment_Answers
    UNION ALL
    SELECT "owner_user_id" AS "user_id", "tags" FROM Answers
    UNION ALL
    SELECT "owner_user_id" AS "user_id", "tags" FROM Questions
) U
JOIN UserIDs UI ON U."user_id" = UI."id";

question: Retrieve the top 50 most viewed questions for each of the following Android-related tags on StackOverflow: 'android-layout', 'android-activity', 'android-intent', 'android-edittext', 'android-fragments', 'android-recyclerview', 'listview', 'android-actionbar', 'google-maps', and 'android-asynctask'. Each question must contain the word 'how' in either its title or body and must not contain any of the following troubleshooting terms in either its title or body: 'fail', 'problem', 'error', 'wrong', 'fix', 'bug', 'issue', 'solve', or 'trouble'. Only include tags that have at least 50 questions meeting these criteria, and for each such tag, select the top 50 questions ranked by view count.
SQL:
WITH filtered_questions AS (
    SELECT 
        "id",
        "title",
        "body",
        "tags",
        "view_count"
    FROM STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.POSTS_QUESTIONS
    WHERE (
        LOWER("title") LIKE '%how%' 
        OR LOWER("body") LIKE '%how%'
    )
    AND NOT (
        LOWER("title") LIKE '%fail%' 
        OR LOWER("body") LIKE '%fail%'
        OR LOWER("title") LIKE '%problem%' 
        OR LOWER("body") LIKE '%problem%'
        OR LOWER("title") LIKE '%error%' 
        OR LOWER("body") LIKE '%error%'
        OR LOWER("title") LIKE '%wrong%' 
        OR LOWER("body") LIKE '%wrong%'
        OR LOWER("title") LIKE '%fix%' 
        OR LOWER("body") LIKE '%fix%'
        OR LOWER("title") LIKE '%bug%' 
        OR LOWER("body") LIKE '%bug%'
        OR LOWER("title") LIKE '%issue%' 
        OR LOWER("body") LIKE '%issue%'
        OR LOWER("title") LIKE '%solve%' 
        OR LOWER("body") LIKE '%solve%'
        OR LOWER("title") LIKE '%trouble%' 
        OR LOWER("body") LIKE '%trouble%'
    )
    AND (
        "tags" ILIKE '%android-layout%' OR
        "tags" ILIKE '%android-activity%' OR
        "tags" ILIKE '%android-intent%' OR
        "tags" ILIKE '%android-edittext%' OR
        "tags" ILIKE '%android-fragments%' OR
        "tags" ILIKE '%android-recyclerview%' OR
        "tags" ILIKE '%listview%' OR
        "tags" ILIKE '%android-actionbar%' OR
        "tags" ILIKE '%google-maps%' OR
        "tags" ILIKE '%android-asynctask%'
    )
),
exploded_tags AS (
    SELECT 
        fq."id",
        fq."title",
        fq."body",
        fq."view_count",
        st.value AS tag
    FROM filtered_questions fq,
         TABLE(SPLIT_TO_TABLE(fq."tags", '|')) AS st
    WHERE st.value IN (
        'android-layout',
        'android-activity',
        'android-intent',
        'android-edittext',
        'android-fragments',
        'android-recyclerview',
        'listview',
        'android-actionbar',
        'google-maps',
        'android-asynctask'
    )
),
tag_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        tag,
        COUNT(*) AS cnt
    FROM exploded_tags
    GROUP BY tag
    HAVING COUNT(*) >= 50
),
ranked_questions AS (
    SELECT 
        et.tag,
        et."id",
        et."title",
        et."body",
        et."view_count",
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY et.tag ORDER BY et."view_count" DESC) AS rn
    FROM exploded_tags et
    INNER JOIN tag_counts tc
      ON et.tag = tc.tag
)
SELECT 
    tag,
    "id",
    "title",
    "body",
    "view_count"
FROM ranked_questions
WHERE rn <= 50
ORDER BY tag, "view_count" DESC;

question: What is the title of the most viewed "how" question related to Android development on StackOverflow, across specified tags such as 'android-layout', 'android-activity', 'android-intent', and others
SQL:
SELECT 
    "title"
FROM 
    STACKOVERFLOW.STACKOVERFLOW.POSTS_QUESTIONS
WHERE 
    LOWER("title") LIKE '%how%'          -- “how” questions
    AND (
           "tags" LIKE '%android-%'      -- covers tags like android-layout, android-activity, android-intent, etc.
        OR "tags" LIKE '%|android|%'     -- covers the base “android” tag when it appears among others
        OR "tags" = 'android'            -- covers questions tagged only with “android”
    )
ORDER BY 
    "view_count" DESC
LIMIT 1;
