    SELECT "user_pseudo_id", "event_date", "event_name" FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180926"  UNION ALL
    SELECT "user_pseudo_id", "event_date", "event_name" FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180927"  UNION ALL
    SELECT "user_pseudo_id", "event_date", "event_name" FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180928"  UNION ALL
    SELECT "user_pseudo_id", "event_date", "event_name" FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180929"  UNION ALL
    SELECT "user_pseudo_id", "event_date", "event_name" FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180930"  UNION ALL
    SELECT "user_pseudo_id", "event_date", "event_name" FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20181001"  UNION ALL
    SELECT "user_pseudo_id", "event_date", "event_name" FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20181002"  UNION ALL
    SELECT "user_pseudo_id", "event_date", "event_name" FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20181003"
),
/* -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Keep only session_start events and convert the date once.
   -------------------------------------------------------------------*/
all_sessions AS
(
    SELECT 
        "user_pseudo_id",
        TO_DATE("event_date", 'YYYYMMDD') AS event_dt
    FROM all_events
    WHERE "event_name" = 'session_start'
),
/* -------------------------------------------------------------------
   First session (first time we see session_start for each user)
   -------------------------------------------------------------------*/
first_session AS
(
    SELECT
        "user_pseudo_id",
        MIN(event_dt) AS first_session_dt
    FROM all_sessions
    GROUP BY "user_pseudo_id"
),
/* -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Build weekly cohorts (Monday as start of week)
   -------------------------------------------------------------------*/
cohort_users AS
(
    SELECT
        DATE_TRUNC('WEEK', first_session_dt) AS cohort_monday,
        "user_pseudo_id"
    FROM first_session
    WHERE first_session_dt >= DATE '2018-07-02'   -- start from 2-Jul-2018
),
/* -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Identify users who return in the 4-th week (days 22-28)
   -------------------------------------------------------------------*/
week4_returns AS
(
    SELECT DISTINCT
        cu.cohort_monday,
        cu."user_pseudo_id"
    FROM cohort_users cu
    JOIN all_sessions s
      ON s."user_pseudo_id" = cu."user_pseudo_id"
     AND s.event_dt >= DATEADD(day, 21, cu.cohort_monday)   -- day 22 (inclusive)
     AND s.event_dt <  DATEADD(day, 28, cu.cohort_monday)   -- before day 29
)
/* -------------------------------------------------------------------
   Calculate 4-th-week retention per cohort and pick the best one
   -------------------------------------------------------------------*/
SELECT TO_CHAR(cohort_monday, 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS cohort_monday
FROM (
        SELECT
            cu.cohort_monday,
            COUNT(DISTINCT wr."user_pseudo_id")                                    AS returning_users,
            COUNT(DISTINCT cu."user_pseudo_id")                                    AS cohort_size,
            COUNT(DISTINCT wr."user_pseudo_id") / CAST(NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT cu."user_pseudo_id"),0) AS FLOAT) AS week4_retention
        FROM cohort_users cu
        LEFT JOIN week4_returns wr
               ON cu.cohort_monday = wr.cohort_monday
              AND cu."user_pseudo_id" = wr."user_pseudo_id"
        GROUP BY cu.cohort_monday
     )
ORDER BY week4_retention DESC, cohort_monday
LIMIT 1;

Calculate the Week 2 retention rate for each weekly cohort, defined as the percentage of users who had a session_start event exactly 2 weeks after their first week. Only include cohorts from July 9, 2018 through September 17, 2018 (the last cohort that can be analyzed for 2-week retention given the available data). Present the results with each weekly cohort and its corresponding Week 2 retention rate, ordered by cohort date.

    SELECT TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD'),"user_pseudo_id","user_first_touch_timestamp","event_name"
      FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180918" UNION ALL
    SELECT TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD'),"user_pseudo_id","user_first_touch_timestamp","event_name"
      FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180919" UNION ALL
    SELECT TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD'),"user_pseudo_id","user_first_touch_timestamp","event_name"
      FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180920" UNION ALL
    SELECT TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD'),"user_pseudo_id","user_first_touch_timestamp","event_name"
      FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180921" UNION ALL
    SELECT TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD'),"user_pseudo_id","user_first_touch_timestamp","event_name"
      FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180922" UNION ALL
    SELECT TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD'),"user_pseudo_id","user_first_touch_timestamp","event_name"
      FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180923" UNION ALL
    SELECT TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD'),"user_pseudo_id","user_first_touch_timestamp","event_name"
      FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180924" UNION ALL
    SELECT TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD'),"user_pseudo_id","user_first_touch_timestamp","event_name"
      FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180925" UNION ALL
    SELECT TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD'),"user_pseudo_id","user_first_touch_timestamp","event_name"
      FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180926" UNION ALL
    SELECT TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD'),"user_pseudo_id","user_first_touch_timestamp","event_name"
      FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180927" UNION ALL
    SELECT TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD'),"user_pseudo_id","user_first_touch_timestamp","event_name"
      FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180928" UNION ALL
    SELECT TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD'),"user_pseudo_id","user_first_touch_timestamp","event_name"
      FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180929" UNION ALL
    SELECT TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD'),"user_pseudo_id","user_first_touch_timestamp","event_name"
      FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180930" UNION ALL
    /* ------------- October (needed for week-2 measurements) ------------- */
    SELECT TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD'),"user_pseudo_id","user_first_touch_timestamp","event_name"
      FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20181001" UNION ALL
    SELECT TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD'),"user_pseudo_id","user_first_touch_timestamp","event_name"
      FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20181002"
),
/* ------------------ identify new users ---------------------- */
first_sessions AS
(
    SELECT DISTINCT
           "user_pseudo_id",
           DATE_TRUNC('WEEK', event_dt) AS cohort_week
    FROM   all_events
    WHERE  "event_name" = 'session_start'
      AND  event_dt = CAST(TO_TIMESTAMP_NTZ("user_first_touch_timestamp" / 1000000) AS DATE)  -- new users
      AND  DATE_TRUNC('WEEK', event_dt) BETWEEN DATE '2018-07-09' AND DATE '2018-09-17'
),
cohort_sizes AS
(
    SELECT  cohort_week,
            COUNT(DISTINCT "user_pseudo_id") AS cohort_users
    FROM    first_sessions
    GROUP BY cohort_week
),
week2_returns AS
(
    SELECT  fs.cohort_week,
            COUNT(DISTINCT fs."user_pseudo_id") AS retained_users
    FROM    first_sessions fs
    JOIN    all_events ev
          ON fs."user_pseudo_id" = ev."user_pseudo_id"
         AND ev."event_name"   = 'session_start'
         AND ev.event_dt       = fs.cohort_week + 14              -- exactly 2 weeks later
    GROUP BY fs.cohort_week
)
/* ------------------- Final result --------------------------- */
SELECT  TO_CHAR(cs.cohort_week, 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS "cohort_week_start",
        ROUND( (COALESCE(w2.retained_users,0) / cs.cohort_users::FLOAT) * 100 , 4 ) AS "week2_retention_rate_percent"
FROM    cohort_sizes cs
LEFT JOIN week2_returns w2
       ON cs.cohort_week = w2.cohort_week
ORDER BY cs.cohort_week;

    UNION ALL SELECT "user_pseudo_id","event_name","event_date" FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180927"
    UNION ALL SELECT "user_pseudo_id","event_name","event_date" FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180928"
    UNION ALL SELECT "user_pseudo_id","event_name","event_date" FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180929"
    UNION ALL SELECT "user_pseudo_id","event_name","event_date" FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20180930"
    UNION ALL SELECT "user_pseudo_id","event_name","event_date" FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20181001"
    UNION ALL SELECT "user_pseudo_id","event_name","event_date" FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282."EVENTS_20181002"
),                                     -- end ALL_EVENTS union
SESSIONS AS (                          -- keep only session_start events
    SELECT  "user_pseudo_id"          AS user_id,
            TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD') AS event_dt
    FROM    ALL_EVENTS
    WHERE   "event_name" = 'session_start'
),
FIRST_SESSIONS AS (                    -- first ever session per user
    SELECT  user_id,
            MIN(event_dt) AS first_session_dt
    FROM    SESSIONS
    GROUP BY user_id
),
COHORT AS (                            -- users whose first ever session is in week starting 2018-07-02
    SELECT  user_id,
            first_session_dt,
            DATE_TRUNC('WEEK', first_session_dt) AS cohort_week_start
    FROM    FIRST_SESSIONS
    WHERE   first_session_dt BETWEEN '2018-07-02' AND '2018-07-08'
),
COHORT_EVENTS AS (                     -- all sessions for cohort users up to 02-Oct-2018
    SELECT  s.user_id,
            DATE_TRUNC('WEEK', s.event_dt)  AS evt_week_start,
            c.cohort_week_start
    FROM    SESSIONS s
    JOIN    COHORT  c
          ON s.user_id = c.user_id
    WHERE   s.event_dt <= '2018-10-02'
),
RETENTION AS (                         -- users retained by week 0-4
    SELECT  DATEDIFF('WEEK', cohort_week_start, evt_week_start) AS week_idx,
            COUNT(DISTINCT user_id)                            AS users_retained
    FROM    COHORT_EVENTS
    WHERE   DATEDIFF('WEEK', cohort_week_start, evt_week_start) BETWEEN 0 AND 4
    GROUP BY week_idx
),
WEEKS AS (                              -- ensure all 5 rows exist
    SELECT  0 AS week_idx, 'Week 0' AS week_lbl UNION ALL
    SELECT  1, 'Week 1' UNION ALL
    SELECT  2, 'Week 2' UNION ALL
    SELECT  3, 'Week 3' UNION ALL
    SELECT  4, 'Week 4'
)
SELECT  w.week_lbl                             AS "WEEK",
        COALESCE(r.users_retained, 0)          AS "USERS_RETAINED"
FROM    WEEKS w
LEFT JOIN RETENTION r
       ON w.week_idx = r.week_idx
ORDER BY w.week_idx;

question: For all users who first opened the app in September 2018 and then uninstalled within seven days, I want to know what percentage of them experienced an app crash (app_exception). The calculation should be done by converting the timestamps to dates first, and then calculating the days to uninstall based on the dates. Only users who uninstalled within 7 days and experienced a crash should be considered in the final percentage.
SQL:
/* ----------------------------------------------------------
   % of September-2018 new users (first_open) that uninstalled
   inside 7 days AND had at least one crash (app_exception)
   ---------------------------------------------------------- */
WITH all_events AS (      --  Union of all daily event tables we need
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180901 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180902 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180903 UNION ALL

    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180908 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180909 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180910 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180911 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180912 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180913 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180914 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180915 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180916 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180917 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180918 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180919 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180920 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180921 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180922 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180923 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180924 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180925 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180926 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180927 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180928 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180929 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20180930 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20181001 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20181002 UNION ALL
    SELECT * FROM FIREBASE.ANALYTICS_153293282.EVENTS_20181003
),
/* 1. Users whose first_open happened in September 2018 */
first_open AS (
    SELECT
        "user_pseudo_id",
        MIN(TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD')) AS first_open_date
    FROM all_events
    WHERE "event_name" = 'first_open'
      AND "event_date" BETWEEN '20180901' AND '20180930'
    GROUP BY "user_pseudo_id"
),
/* 2. First uninstall date for every user (if any) */
uninstall AS (
    SELECT
        "user_pseudo_id",
        MIN(TO_DATE("event_date",'YYYYMMDD')) AS uninstall_date
    FROM all_events
    WHERE "event_name" = 'app_remove'
    GROUP BY "user_pseudo_id"
),
/* 3. Users whose uninstall happened within 7 days after first_open */
cohort AS (
    SELECT
        fo."user_pseudo_id",
        fo.first_open_date,
        un.uninstall_date
    FROM first_open        AS fo
    JOIN uninstall         AS un
          ON fo."user_pseudo_id" = un."user_pseudo_id"
    WHERE DATEDIFF(day , fo.first_open_date , un.uninstall_date) BETWEEN 0 AND 7
),
/* 4. Users in that cohort who had at least one crash (app_exception) within the 7-day window */
crashes AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT a."user_pseudo_id"
    FROM all_events a
    JOIN cohort c
      ON a."user_pseudo_id" = c."user_pseudo_id"
    WHERE a."event_name" = 'app_exception'
      AND TO_DATE(a."event_date",'YYYYMMDD') BETWEEN c.first_open_date
                                                 AND DATEADD(day , 7 , c.first_open_date)
)
SELECT
    ROUND(
        COUNT(DISTINCT cr."user_pseudo_id") * 100.0
        / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT co."user_pseudo_id"),0)
    , 4)           AS "PERCENT_USERS_WITH_CRASH"
FROM cohort  AS co
LEFT JOIN crashes AS cr
       ON co."user_pseudo_id" = cr."user_pseudo_id";

question: Can you calculate the 5-day symmetric moving average of predicted toy sales for December 5 to 8, 2018, using daily sales data from January 1, 2017, to August 29, 2018, with a simple linear regression model? Finally provide the sum of those four 5-day moving averages?
SQL:
WITH daily_toy_sales AS (
    SELECT
        TO_DATE("ORDERS"."order_purchase_timestamp") AS "order_date",
        SUM("ORDER_ITEMS"."price") AS "daily_sales"
    FROM
        "E_COMMERCE"."E_COMMERCE"."ORDER_ITEMS"
        INNER JOIN "E_COMMERCE"."E_COMMERCE"."ORDERS"
            ON "ORDER_ITEMS"."order_id" = "ORDERS"."order_id"
        INNER JOIN "E_COMMERCE"."E_COMMERCE"."PRODUCTS"
            ON "ORDER_ITEMS"."product_id" = "PRODUCTS"."product_id"
        INNER JOIN "E_COMMERCE"."E_COMMERCE"."PRODUCT_CATEGORY_NAME_TRANSLATION"
            ON "PRODUCTS"."product_category_name" = "PRODUCT_CATEGORY_NAME_TRANSLATION"."product_category_name"
    WHERE
        "PRODUCT_CATEGORY_NAME_TRANSLATION"."product_category_name_english" ILIKE '%toy%'
        AND TO_DATE("ORDERS"."order_purchase_timestamp") >= '2017-01-01'
        AND TO_DATE("ORDERS"."order_purchase_timestamp") <= '2018-08-29'
    GROUP BY
        TO_DATE("ORDERS"."order_purchase_timestamp")
),
daily_toy_sales_with_seq AS (
    SELECT
        "order_date",
        "daily_sales",
        DATEDIFF(day, '2017-01-01', "order_date") AS "day_seq"
    FROM
        daily_toy_sales
),
regression_stats AS (
    SELECT
        COUNT(*) AS n,
        SUM("day_seq") AS sum_x,
        SUM("daily_sales") AS sum_y,
        SUM("day_seq" * "daily_sales") AS sum_xy,
        SUM("day_seq" * "day_seq") AS sum_xx
    FROM
        daily_toy_sales_with_seq
),
regression_coeff AS (
    SELECT
        (
            (sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y / n) /
            (sum_xx - (sum_x * sum_x) / n)
        ) AS slope,
        (
            (sum_y / n) -
            (
                (sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y / n) /
                (sum_xx - (sum_x * sum_x) / n)
            ) * (sum_x / n)
        ) AS intercept
    FROM
        regression_stats
),
-- Correct the generator usage: It's ROW_NUMBER, not "SEQ".
date_seq_to_predict AS (
    SELECT
        DATEADD(day, row_number - 1, TO_DATE('2018-12-01')) AS seq_date,
        DATEDIFF(day, '2017-01-01', DATEADD(day, row_number - 1, TO_DATE('2018-12-01'))) AS day_seq
    FROM (
        SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY SEQ4()) AS row_number
        FROM TABLE(GENERATOR(ROWCOUNT => 12))
    )
),
predicted_sales AS (
    SELECT
        seq_date,
        intercept + slope * day_seq AS predicted_daily_sales
    FROM
        date_seq_to_predict, regression_coeff
),
moving_windows AS (
    SELECT
        center_date,
        LAG(center_date, 2) OVER (ORDER BY center_date) AS d_minus2,
        LAG(center_date, 1) OVER (ORDER BY center_date) AS d_minus1,
        center_date AS d_center,
        LEAD(center_date, 1) OVER (ORDER BY center_date) AS d_plus1,
        LEAD(center_date, 2) OVER (ORDER BY center_date) AS d_plus2
    FROM (
        SELECT TO_DATE('2018-12-05') AS center_date UNION ALL
        SELECT TO_DATE('2018-12-06') UNION ALL
        SELECT TO_DATE('2018-12-07') UNION ALL
        SELECT TO_DATE('2018-12-08')
    )
),
windows_long AS (
    SELECT
        mw.center_date,
        ps.seq_date,
        ps.predicted_daily_sales
    FROM
        moving_windows mw
        JOIN predicted_sales ps
            ON ps.seq_date IN (
                mw.d_minus2,
                mw.d_minus1,
                mw.d_center,
                mw.d_plus1,
                mw.d_plus2
            )
),
window_avg AS (
    SELECT
        center_date,
        AVG(predicted_daily_sales) AS symmetric_5day_ma
    FROM
        windows_long
    GROUP BY
        center_date
)
SELECT
    ROUND(SUM(symmetric_5day_ma), 4) AS sum_5day_symmetric_mas
FROM
    window_avg
;

question: According to the RFM definition document, calculate the average sales per order for each customer within distinct RFM segments, considering only 'delivered' orders. Use the customer unique identifier. Clearly define how to calculate Recency based on the latest purchase timestamp and specify the criteria for classifying RFM segments. The average sales should be computed as the total spend divided by the total number of orders. Please analyze and report the differences in average sales across the RFM segments
SQL:
WITH CustomerOrderStats AS (
    SELECT
        C."customer_unique_id",
        O."order_id",
        TO_TIMESTAMP(O."order_purchase_timestamp") AS "order_purchase_timestamp",
        P."payment_value"
    FROM "E_COMMERCE"."E_COMMERCE"."CUSTOMERS" AS C
    JOIN "E_COMMERCE"."E_COMMERCE"."ORDERS" AS O
        ON C."customer_id" = O."customer_id"
    JOIN "E_COMMERCE"."E_COMMERCE"."ORDER_PAYMENTS" AS P
        ON O."order_id" = P."order_id"
    WHERE O."order_status" = 'delivered'
),
MaxDate AS (
    SELECT MAX("order_purchase_timestamp") AS "max_purchase_date" FROM CustomerOrderStats
),
RFM_Base AS (
    SELECT
        "customer_unique_id",
        DATEDIFF(day, MAX("order_purchase_timestamp"), (SELECT "max_purchase_date" FROM MaxDate)) AS "Recency",
        COUNT(DISTINCT "order_id") AS "Frequency",
        SUM("payment_value") AS "Monetary",
        COUNT(DISTINCT "order_id") AS "TotalOrders",
        SUM("payment_value") AS "TotalSpend"
    FROM CustomerOrderStats
    GROUP BY "customer_unique_id"
),
RFM_Scores AS (
    SELECT
        *,
        NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY "Recency" ASC) AS "R",
        NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY "Frequency" DESC) AS "F",
        NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY "Monetary" DESC) AS "M"
    FROM RFM_Base
),
RFM_Segments AS (
    SELECT
        *,
        CASE
            WHEN "R" = 1 AND ("F" + "M") BETWEEN 1 AND 4 THEN 'Champions'
            WHEN ("R" = 4 OR "R" = 5) AND ("F" + "M") BETWEEN 1 AND 2 THEN 'Can''t Lose Them'
            WHEN ("R" = 4 OR "R" = 5) AND ("F" + "M") BETWEEN 3 AND 6 THEN 'Hibernating'
            WHEN ("R" = 4 OR "R" = 5) AND ("F" + "M") BETWEEN 7 AND 10 THEN 'Lost'
            WHEN ("R" = 2 OR "R" = 3) AND ("F" + "M") BETWEEN 1 AND 4 THEN 'Loyal Customers'
            WHEN "R" = 3 AND ("F" + "M") BETWEEN 5 AND 6 THEN 'Needs Attention'
            WHEN "R" = 1 AND ("F" + "M") BETWEEN 7 AND 8 THEN 'Recent Users'
            WHEN ("R" = 1 AND ("F" + "M") BETWEEN 5 AND 6) OR ("R" = 2 AND ("F" + "M") BETWEEN 5 AND 8) THEN 'Potential Loyalists'
            WHEN "R" = 1 AND ("F" + "M") BETWEEN 9 AND 10 THEN 'Price Sensitive'
            WHEN "R" = 2 AND ("F" + "M") BETWEEN 9 AND 10 THEN 'Promising'
            WHEN "R" = 3 AND ("F" + "M") BETWEEN 7 AND 10 THEN 'About to Sleep'
            ELSE 'Others'
        END AS "RFM_Segment"
    FROM RFM_Scores
)
SELECT
    "RFM_Segment",
    SUM("TotalSpend") / SUM("TotalOrders") AS "AverageSalesPerOrder"
FROM RFM_Segments
GROUP BY "RFM_Segment"
ORDER BY "AverageSalesPerOrder" DESC

question: Could you tell me the number of orders, average payment per order and customer lifespan in weeks of the 3 custumers with the highest average payment per order, where the lifespan is calculated by subtracting the earliest purchase date from the latest purchase date in days, dividing by seven, and if the result is less than seven days, setting it to 1.0?
SQL:
WITH OrderTotalPayments AS (
    SELECT
        "order_id",
        SUM("payment_value") AS "total_payment"
    FROM "E_COMMERCE"."E_COMMERCE"."ORDER_PAYMENTS"
    GROUP BY "order_id"
)
SELECT
    C."customer_unique_id",
    COUNT(O."order_id") AS "number_of_orders",
    AVG(OTP."total_payment") AS "average_payment_per_order",
    CASE
        WHEN DATEDIFF('day', MIN(TO_TIMESTAMP(O."order_purchase_timestamp")), MAX(TO_TIMESTAMP(O."order_purchase_timestamp"))) < 7
        THEN 1.0
        ELSE DATEDIFF('day', MIN(TO_TIMESTAMP(O."order_purchase_timestamp")), MAX(TO_TIMESTAMP(O."order_purchase_timestamp"))) / 7.0
    END AS "customer_lifespan_in_weeks"
FROM "E_COMMERCE"."E_COMMERCE"."CUSTOMERS" AS C
JOIN "E_COMMERCE"."E_COMMERCE"."ORDERS" AS O ON C."customer_id" = O."customer_id"
JOIN OrderTotalPayments AS OTP ON O."order_id" = OTP."order_id"
GROUP BY C."customer_unique_id"
ORDER BY "average_payment_per_order" DESC
LIMIT 3;

question: Could you help me calculate the average single career span value in years for all baseball players? Please precise the result as a float number. First, calculate the difference in years, months, and days between the debut and final game dates. For each player, the career span is computed as the sum of the absolute number of years, plus the absolute number of months divided by 12, plus the absolute number of days divided by 365. Round each part to two decimal places before summing. Finally, average the career spans and round the result to a float number.
SQL:
WITH base AS (   -- 1. Read dates with safe conversion
    SELECT
        player_id,
        TRY_TO_DATE(debut)      AS debut_dt,
        TRY_TO_DATE(final_game) AS final_dt
    FROM BASEBALL.BASEBALL.PLAYER
),
filtered AS (     -- 2. Keep only rows with valid dates
    SELECT *
    FROM base
    WHERE debut_dt IS NOT NULL
      AND final_dt IS NOT NULL
),
mnths AS (        -- 3. Total complete months (absolute) between the two dates
    SELECT
        player_id,
        ABS(DATEDIFF('month', debut_dt, final_dt)) AS tot_months,
        LEAST(debut_dt, final_dt)  AS start_dt,
        GREATEST(debut_dt, final_dt) AS end_dt
    FROM filtered
),
parts AS (        -- 4. Split into year/month/day components
    SELECT
        player_id,
        FLOOR(tot_months / 12)                       AS yr_part,      -- years
        MOD(tot_months, 12)                          AS mo_part,      -- months
        DATEDIFF(
            'day',
            DATEADD('month', tot_months, start_dt),
            end_dt
        )                                            AS day_part      -- days
    FROM mnths
),
spans AS (        -- 5. Compute each player's span (round each piece first)
    SELECT
        player_id,
        ROUND(yr_part :: NUMBER, 2) +
        ROUND(mo_part / 12.0, 2) +
        ROUND(day_part / 365.0, 2) AS career_span
    FROM parts
)
-- 6. Average career span (rounded to 4 decimals as requested)
SELECT ROUND(AVG(career_span), 4) AS average_career_span
FROM spans;

question: I would like to know the given names of baseball players who have achieved the highest value of games played, runs, hits, and home runs, with their corresponding score values.
SQL:
WITH "BATTING_TOTALS" AS (
    SELECT
        "PLAYER_ID",
        SUM(COALESCE("G", 0))                                           AS "TOTAL_G",
        SUM(COALESCE(TRY_TO_NUMBER("R"), 0))                            AS "TOTAL_R",
        SUM(COALESCE(TRY_TO_NUMBER("H"), 0))                            AS "TOTAL_H",
        SUM(COALESCE(TRY_TO_NUMBER("HR"), 0))                           AS "TOTAL_HR"
    FROM BASEBALL.BASEBALL.BATTING
    GROUP BY "PLAYER_ID"
),
"MAX_VALUES" AS (
    SELECT
        MAX("TOTAL_G")  AS "MAX_G",
        MAX("TOTAL_R")  AS "MAX_R",
        MAX("TOTAL_H")  AS "MAX_H",
        MAX("TOTAL_HR") AS "MAX_HR"
    FROM "BATTING_TOTALS"
)
SELECT 'Games Played' AS "METRIC",
       p."NAME_GIVEN" AS "PLAYER_GIVEN_NAME",
       b."TOTAL_G"    AS "VALUE"
FROM "BATTING_TOTALS" b
JOIN "MAX_VALUES" mv ON b."TOTAL_G" = mv."MAX_G"
JOIN BASEBALL.BASEBALL.PLAYER p ON p."PLAYER_ID" = b."PLAYER_ID"

UNION ALL

SELECT 'Runs'        AS "METRIC",
       p."NAME_GIVEN" AS "PLAYER_GIVEN_NAME",
       b."TOTAL_R"    AS "VALUE"
FROM "BATTING_TOTALS" b
JOIN "MAX_VALUES" mv ON b."TOTAL_R" = mv."MAX_R"
JOIN BASEBALL.BASEBALL.PLAYER p ON p."PLAYER_ID" = b."PLAYER_ID"

UNION ALL

SELECT 'Hits'        AS "METRIC",
       p."NAME_GIVEN" AS "PLAYER_GIVEN_NAME",
       b."TOTAL_H"    AS "VALUE"
FROM "BATTING_TOTALS" b
JOIN "MAX_VALUES" mv ON b."TOTAL_H" = mv."MAX_H"
JOIN BASEBALL.BASEBALL.PLAYER p ON p."PLAYER_ID" = b."PLAYER_ID"

UNION ALL

SELECT 'Home Runs'   AS "METRIC",
       p."NAME_GIVEN" AS "PLAYER_GIVEN_NAME",
       b."TOTAL_HR"   AS "VALUE"
FROM "BATTING_TOTALS" b
JOIN "MAX_VALUES" mv ON b."TOTAL_HR" = mv."MAX_HR"
JOIN BASEBALL.BASEBALL.PLAYER p ON p."PLAYER_ID" = b."PLAYER_ID"

ORDER BY "METRIC";

question: What is the distance of the longest route where Abakan is either the departure or destination city (in kilometers)?
SQL:
WITH flights_abakan AS (
  SELECT
    f."flight_id",
    dep."airport_code" AS "dep_code",
    arr."airport_code" AS "arr_code",
    TRY_PARSE_JSON(dep."city"):"en"::string AS "dep_city",
    TRY_PARSE_JSON(arr."city"):"en"::string AS "arr_city",
    dep."coordinates" AS "dep_coord",
    arr."coordinates" AS "arr_coord"
  FROM "AIRLINES"."AIRLINES"."FLIGHTS" AS f
  JOIN "AIRLINES"."AIRLINES"."AIRPORTS_DATA" AS dep
    ON dep."airport_code" = f."departure_airport"
  JOIN "AIRLINES"."AIRLINES"."AIRPORTS_DATA" AS arr
    ON arr."airport_code" = f."arrival_airport"
  WHERE TRY_PARSE_JSON(dep."city"):"en"::string = 'Abakan'
     OR TRY_PARSE_JSON(arr."city"):"en"::string = 'Abakan'
),
coords AS (
  SELECT
    "flight_id",
    CAST(SPLIT_PART(REPLACE(REPLACE("dep_coord",'(',''),')',''), ',', 1) AS DOUBLE) AS "dep_lon",
    CAST(SPLIT_PART(REPLACE(REPLACE("dep_coord",'(',''),')',''), ',', 2) AS DOUBLE) AS "dep_lat",
    CAST(SPLIT_PART(REPLACE(REPLACE("arr_coord",'(',''),')',''), ',', 1) AS DOUBLE) AS "arr_lon",
    CAST(SPLIT_PART(REPLACE(REPLACE("arr_coord",'(',''),')',''), ',', 2) AS DOUBLE) AS "arr_lat"
  FROM flights_abakan
),
dist AS (
  SELECT
    "flight_id",
    6371 * 2 * ASIN(
      SQRT(
        POWER(SIN((RADIANS("arr_lat") - RADIANS("dep_lat")) / 2), 2)
        + COS(RADIANS("dep_lat")) * COS(RADIANS("arr_lat")) * POWER(SIN((RADIANS("arr_lon") - RADIANS("dep_lon")) / 2), 2)
      )
    ) AS "distance_km"
  FROM coords
)
SELECT MAX("distance_km") AS "max_distance_km"
FROM dist;

question: Distribute all the unique city pairs into the distance ranges 0, 1000, 2000, 3000, 4000, 5000, and 6000+, based on their average distance of all routes between them. Then how many pairs are there in the distance range with the fewest unique city paires?
SQL:
WITH FLIGHT_INFO AS (
    SELECT    
        FLIGHTS."flight_id",
        PARSE_JSON(DEPARTURE."city"):"en" AS "from_city",
        CAST(SUBSTR(DEPARTURE."coordinates", 2, POSITION(',' IN DEPARTURE."coordinates") - 2) AS DOUBLE) AS "from_longitude",
        CAST(SUBSTR(DEPARTURE."coordinates", POSITION(',' IN DEPARTURE."coordinates") + 1, LENGTH(DEPARTURE."coordinates") - POSITION(',' IN DEPARTURE."coordinates") - 2) AS DOUBLE) AS "from_latitude",
        PARSE_JSON(ARRIVAL."city"):"en" AS "to_city",
        CAST(SUBSTR(ARRIVAL."coordinates", 2, POSITION(',' IN ARRIVAL."coordinates") - 2) AS DOUBLE) AS "to_longitude",
        CAST(SUBSTR(ARRIVAL."coordinates", POSITION(',' IN ARRIVAL."coordinates") + 1, LENGTH(ARRIVAL."coordinates") - POSITION(',' IN ARRIVAL."coordinates") - 2) AS DOUBLE) AS "to_latitude"
    FROM
        AIRLINES.AIRLINES.FLIGHTS 
    LEFT JOIN AIRLINES.AIRLINES.AIRPORTS_DATA AS DEPARTURE ON FLIGHTS."departure_airport" = DEPARTURE."airport_code"
    LEFT JOIN AIRLINES.AIRLINES.AIRPORTS_DATA AS ARRIVAL ON FLIGHTS."arrival_airport" = ARRIVAL."airport_code"
)
-- Create a histogram distribution of average_distance_km
SELECT "group_count" FROM
(
    SELECT
        FLOOR("average_distance_km" / 1000) * 1000 AS "distance_range",
        COUNT(*) AS "group_count"
    FROM (
        -- Calculate the average distance for each unique combination of from_city and to_city
        SELECT
            "from_city",
            "to_city",
            AVG("distance_km") AS "average_distance_km"
        FROM (
            -- Subquery to calculate the distances as before
            SELECT
                "from_city",
                "to_city",
                -- Calculate the distance using the Haversine formula
                2 * 6371 * ASIN(SQRT(
                    POWER(SIN(RADIANS(("to_latitude" - "from_latitude") / 2)), 2) +
                    COS(RADIANS("from_latitude")) * COS(RADIANS("to_latitude")) *
                    POWER(SIN(RADIANS(("to_longitude" - "from_longitude") / 2)), 2)
                )) AS "distance_km"
            FROM FLIGHT_INFO
        ) AS subquery
        GROUP BY "from_city", "to_city"
    ) AS distances
    GROUP BY "distance_range"
    ORDER BY "group_count"
    LIMIT 1
)

question: Please calculate the fatality rate for motorcycle collisions, separated by helmet usage. Specifically, calculate two percentages: 1) the percentage of motorcyclist fatalities in collisions where parties (drivers or passengers) were wearing helmets, and 2) the percentage of motorcyclist fatalities in collisions where parties were not wearing helmets. For each group, compute this by dividing the total number of motorcyclist fatalities by the total number of collisions involving that group. Use the parties table to determine helmet usage (from party_safety_equipment fields).
SQL:
WITH "moto_collisions" AS (
  SELECT
    "case_id",
    COALESCE("motorcyclist_killed_count", 0) AS "motorcyclist_killed_count"
  FROM "CALIFORNIA_TRAFFIC_COLLISION"."CALIFORNIA_TRAFFIC_COLLISION"."COLLISIONS"
  WHERE COALESCE("motorcycle_collision", 0) = 1
),
"moto_party_helmet" AS (
  SELECT
    p."case_id",
    MAX(
      CASE
        WHEN (
          (LOWER(COALESCE(p."party_safety_equipment_1", '')) LIKE '%helmet%' AND LOWER(COALESCE(p."party_safety_equipment_1", '')) NOT LIKE '%not%' AND LOWER(COALESCE(p."party_safety_equipment_1", '')) NOT LIKE '%no helmet%')
          OR
          (LOWER(COALESCE(p."party_safety_equipment_2", '')) LIKE '%helmet%' AND LOWER(COALESCE(p."party_safety_equipment_2", '')) NOT LIKE '%not%' AND LOWER(COALESCE(p."party_safety_equipment_2", '')) NOT LIKE '%no helmet%')
        ) THEN 1 ELSE 0
      END
    ) AS "worn_any",
    MAX(
      CASE
        WHEN (
          (LOWER(COALESCE(p."party_safety_equipment_1", '')) LIKE '%helmet%' AND (LOWER(COALESCE(p."party_safety_equipment_1", '')) LIKE '%not%' OR LOWER(COALESCE(p."party_safety_equipment_1", '')) LIKE '%no helmet%'))
          OR
          (LOWER(COALESCE(p."party_safety_equipment_2", '')) LIKE '%helmet%' AND (LOWER(COALESCE(p."party_safety_equipment_2", '')) LIKE '%not%' OR LOWER(COALESCE(p."party_safety_equipment_2", '')) LIKE '%no helmet%'))
        ) THEN 1 ELSE 0
      END
    ) AS "not_worn_any"
  FROM "CALIFORNIA_TRAFFIC_COLLISION"."CALIFORNIA_TRAFFIC_COLLISION"."PARTIES" p
  INNER JOIN "moto_collisions" mc
    ON mc."case_id" = p."case_id"
  WHERE LOWER(COALESCE(p."statewide_vehicle_type", '')) LIKE '%motorcycle%'
  GROUP BY p."case_id"
),
"classified" AS (
  SELECT
    mc."case_id",
    mc."motorcyclist_killed_count",
    CASE
      WHEN mph."worn_any" = 1 AND COALESCE(mph."not_worn_any", 0) = 0 THEN 'helmet_worn'
      WHEN mph."not_worn_any" = 1 AND COALESCE(mph."worn_any", 0) = 0 THEN 'no_helmet'
      ELSE NULL
    END AS "helmet_group"
  FROM "moto_collisions" mc
  LEFT JOIN "moto_party_helmet" mph
    ON mc."case_id" = mph."case_id"
)
SELECT
  'helmet_worn' AS "helmet_usage",
  COALESCE(
    100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN "helmet_group" = 'helmet_worn' THEN "motorcyclist_killed_count" ELSE 0 END)
    / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN "helmet_group" = 'helmet_worn' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0),
    0
  ) AS "fatality_percentage"
FROM "classified"
UNION ALL
SELECT
  'no_helmet' AS "helmet_usage",
  COALESCE(
    100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN "helmet_group" = 'no_helmet' THEN "motorcyclist_killed_count" ELSE 0 END)
    / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN "helmet_group" = 'no_helmet' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0),
    0
  ) AS "fatality_percentage"
FROM "classified";

question: In which year were the two most common causes of traffic accidents different from those in other years?
SQL:
WITH collisions_year AS (
    SELECT
        ci."db_year",
        c."pcf_violation_category"
    FROM
        "CALIFORNIA_TRAFFIC_COLLISION"."CALIFORNIA_TRAFFIC_COLLISION"."COLLISIONS" c
        INNER JOIN "CALIFORNIA_TRAFFIC_COLLISION"."CALIFORNIA_TRAFFIC_COLLISION"."CASE_IDS" ci
            ON c."case_id" = ci."case_id"
    WHERE
        c."pcf_violation_category" IS NOT NULL
        AND c."pcf_violation_category" <> ''
),
top2_per_year AS (
    SELECT
        "db_year",
        "pcf_violation_category",
        COUNT(*) AS "count"
    FROM collisions_year
    GROUP BY "db_year", "pcf_violation_category"
),
ranked_causes AS (
    SELECT
        "db_year",
        "pcf_violation_category",
        "count",
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY "db_year" ORDER BY "count" DESC) AS rk
    FROM top2_per_year
),
top2_causes_by_year AS (
    SELECT
        "db_year",
        LISTAGG("pcf_violation_category", ',') WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "pcf_violation_category") AS "top2_cause_str"
    FROM ranked_causes
    WHERE rk <= 2
    GROUP BY "db_year"
),
most_common_top2 AS (
    SELECT
        "top2_cause_str"
    FROM top2_causes_by_year
    GROUP BY "top2_cause_str"
    ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
    y."db_year"
FROM
    top2_causes_by_year y,
    most_common_top2 m
WHERE
    y."top2_cause_str" <> m."top2_cause_str"
ORDER BY y."db_year";

question: For the primary collision factor violation category that was the most common cause of traffic accidents in 2021, how many percentage points did its share of annual road incidents in 2021 decrease compared to its share in 2011?
SQL:
WITH collisions_11_21 AS (          -- keep only 2011- and 2021-dated crashes
    SELECT 
        SUBSTR("collision_date", 1, 4)        AS "yr",
        "pcf_violation_category"
    FROM   CALIFORNIA_TRAFFIC_COLLISION.CALIFORNIA_TRAFFIC_COLLISION."COLLISIONS"
    WHERE  "collision_date" LIKE '2011-%'
        OR "collision_date" LIKE '2021-%'
),
cnts AS (                            -- count by (year , category)
    SELECT 
        "yr",
        "pcf_violation_category",
        COUNT(*)                     AS "cnt"
    FROM   collisions_11_21
    GROUP  BY "yr", "pcf_violation_category"
),
top_cat AS (                         -- most frequent category in 2021
    SELECT "pcf_violation_category" AS "category"
    FROM   cnts
    WHERE  "yr" = '2021'
    ORDER  BY "cnt" DESC NULLS LAST
    LIMIT  1
),
year_totals AS (                     -- total collisions per year
    SELECT "yr", SUM("cnt") AS "tot_cnt"
    FROM   cnts
    GROUP  BY "yr"
),
cat_totals AS (                      -- collisions of that top category per year
    SELECT 
        c."yr",
        c."cnt"                      AS "cat_cnt"
    FROM   cnts c
    JOIN   top_cat t
           ON c."pcf_violation_category" = t."category"
)
SELECT 
    ROUND( 
        (SELECT ct."cat_cnt"::FLOAT / yt."tot_cnt" * 100
           FROM cat_totals ct
           JOIN year_totals yt ON yt."yr" = ct."yr"
          WHERE ct."yr" = '2011')
      - 
        (SELECT ct."cat_cnt"::FLOAT / yt."tot_cnt" * 100
           FROM cat_totals ct
           JOIN year_totals yt ON yt."yr" = ct."yr"
          WHERE ct."yr" = '2021')
    , 4) AS "percentage_point_decrease";

question: For the NXT title that had the shortest match (excluding titles with "title change"), what were the names of the two wrestlers involved?
SQL:
SELECT w1."name" as wrestler1, w2."name" as wrestler2
FROM WWE.WWE.MATCHES m
JOIN WWE.WWE.WRESTLERS w1 ON m."winner_id" = w1."id"::VARCHAR
JOIN WWE.WWE.WRESTLERS w2 ON m."loser_id" = w2."id"::VARCHAR
WHERE m."title_id" IN (SELECT "id"::VARCHAR FROM WWE.WWE.BELTS WHERE "name" LIKE '%NXT%') 
AND m."title_change" = 0 
AND m."duration" IS NOT NULL 
AND m."duration" != '' 
ORDER BY m."duration" ASC 
LIMIT 1

question: Please help me identify the top 3 bowlers who, in the overs where the maximum runs were conceded in each match, gave up the highest number of runs in a single over across all matches. For each of these bowlers, provide the match in which they conceded these maximum runs. Only consider overs that had the most runs conceded within their respective matches, and among these, determine which bowlers conceded the most runs in a single over overall.
SQL:
-- Status: Finished in turn 6.

WITH over_runs AS (
  SELECT 
    "bbb"."match_id",
    "bbb"."over_id",
    "bbb"."bowler",
    COALESCE(SUM("bs"."runs_scored"), 0) + COALESCE(SUM("er"."extra_runs"), 0) AS "total_runs_conceded"
  FROM "IPL"."IPL"."BALL_BY_BALL" "bbb"
  LEFT JOIN "IPL"."IPL"."BATSMAN_SCORED" "bs" 
    ON "bbb"."match_id" = "bs"."match_id" 
    AND "bbb"."innings_no" = "bs"."innings_no"
    AND "bbb"."over_id" = "bs"."over_id"
    AND "bbb"."ball_id" = "bs"."ball_id"
  LEFT JOIN "IPL"."IPL"."EXTRA_RUNS" "er"
    ON "bbb"."match_id" = "er"."match_id"
    AND "bbb"."innings_no" = "er"."innings_no" 
    AND "bbb"."over_id" = "er"."over_id"
    AND "bbb"."ball_id" = "er"."ball_id"
  GROUP BY "bbb"."match_id", "bbb"."over_id", "bbb"."bowler"
),
max_runs_per_match AS (
  SELECT 
    "match_id",
    MAX("total_runs_conceded") AS "max_runs_conceded_in_single_over"
  FROM over_runs
  GROUP BY "match_id"
),
worst_bowlers_per_match AS (
  SELECT 
    "or"."match_id",
    "or"."bowler",
    "or"."over_id",
    "or"."total_runs_conceded"
  FROM over_runs "or"
  INNER JOIN max_runs_per_match "mrpm"
    ON "or"."match_id" = "mrpm"."match_id"
    AND "or"."total_runs_conceded" = "mrpm"."max_runs_conceded_in_single_over"
),
top_3_worst_bowlers AS (
  SELECT 
    "match_id",
    "bowler",
    "over_id",
    "total_runs_conceded",
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY "total_runs_conceded" DESC) AS "rank"
  FROM worst_bowlers_per_match
)
SELECT 
  "p"."player_name",
  "t3wb"."total_runs_conceded",
  "t3wb"."match_id"
FROM top_3_worst_bowlers "t3wb"
INNER JOIN "IPL"."IPL"."PLAYER" "p"
  ON "t3wb"."bowler" = "p"."player_id"
WHERE "t3wb"."rank" <= 3

question: Which bowler has the lowest bowling average per wicket taken?
SQL:
WITH Total_Runs AS (
    SELECT 
        b."bowler",
        SUM(bs."runs_scored" + COALESCE(er."extra_runs", 0)) AS total_runs
    FROM 
        IPL.IPL.BALL_BY_BALL b
    LEFT JOIN 
        IPL.IPL.BATSMAN_SCORED bs
    ON 
        b."match_id" = bs."match_id" AND
        b."over_id" = bs."over_id" AND
        b."ball_id" = bs."ball_id" AND
        b."innings_no" = bs."innings_no"
    LEFT JOIN 
        IPL.IPL.EXTRA_RUNS er
    ON 
        b."match_id" = er."match_id" AND
        b."over_id" = er."over_id" AND
        b."ball_id" = er."ball_id" AND
        b."innings_no" = er."innings_no"
    GROUP BY
        b."bowler"
),

Wickets_Taken AS (
    SELECT 
        b."bowler",
        COUNT(wt."player_out") AS total_wickets
    FROM 
        IPL.IPL.BALL_BY_BALL b
    JOIN 
        IPL.IPL.WICKET_TAKEN wt
    ON 
        b."match_id" = wt."match_id" AND
        b."over_id" = wt."over_id" AND
        b."ball_id" = wt."ball_id" AND
        b."innings_no" = wt."innings_no"
    GROUP BY 
        b."bowler"
)

SELECT 
    p."player_name",
    Total_Runs."bowler",
    COALESCE(Total_Runs.total_runs / NULLIF(Wickets_Taken.total_wickets, 0), 0) AS bowling_average
FROM 
    Total_Runs
JOIN 
    Wickets_Taken
ON 
    Total_Runs."bowler" = Wickets_Taken."bowler"
JOIN 
    IPL.IPL.PLAYER p
ON 
    Total_Runs."bowler" = p."player_id"
ORDER BY 
    bowling_average ASC
LIMIT 1;

question: Could you calculate the average of the total runs scored by all strikers who have scored more than 50 runs in any single match?
SQL:
WITH runs_per_player_match AS (
    /* Runs scored by every striker in each match */
    SELECT
        bb."striker"      AS "player_id",
        bb."match_id",
        SUM(bs."runs_scored") AS "runs_in_match"
    FROM "IPL"."IPL"."BALL_BY_BALL"   bb
    JOIN "IPL"."IPL"."BATSMAN_SCORED" bs
         ON  bb."match_id"   = bs."match_id"
         AND bb."innings_no" = bs."innings_no"
         AND bb."over_id"    = bs."over_id"
         AND bb."ball_id"    = bs."ball_id"
    GROUP BY
        bb."striker",
        bb."match_id"
),
qualified_players AS (
    /* Strikers who scored more than 50 in at least one match */
    SELECT DISTINCT
        "player_id"
    FROM runs_per_player_match
    WHERE "runs_in_match" > 50
),
total_runs_per_player AS (
    /* Total (career) runs for each qualified striker */
    SELECT
        rpm."player_id",
        SUM(rpm."runs_in_match") AS "total_runs"
    FROM runs_per_player_match rpm
    JOIN qualified_players qp
      ON rpm."player_id" = qp."player_id"
    GROUP BY
        rpm."player_id"
)
SELECT
    ROUND(AVG("total_runs"), 4) AS "average_total_runs"
FROM total_runs_per_player;

question: Retrieve the names of players who scored no less than 100 runs in a match while playing for the team that lost that match.
SQL:
with "PLAYER_RUNS" as (
  select
    "B"."match_id",
    "B"."striker" as "player_id",
    sum("BS"."runs_scored") as "runs"
  from "IPL"."IPL"."BALL_BY_BALL" "B"
  join "IPL"."IPL"."BATSMAN_SCORED" "BS"
    on "B"."match_id" = "BS"."match_id"
   and "B"."over_id" = "BS"."over_id"
   and "B"."ball_id" = "BS"."ball_id"
   and "B"."innings_no" = "BS"."innings_no"
  group by "B"."match_id", "B"."striker"
)
select distinct "P"."player_name"
from "PLAYER_RUNS" "PR"
join "IPL"."IPL"."PLAYER_MATCH" "PM"
  on "PM"."match_id" = "PR"."match_id"
 and "PM"."player_id" = "PR"."player_id"
join "IPL"."IPL"."MATCH" "M"
  on "M"."match_id" = "PR"."match_id"
join "IPL"."IPL"."PLAYER" "P"
  on "P"."player_id" = "PR"."player_id"
where "PR"."runs" >= 100
  and "M"."match_winner" is not null
  and "PM"."team_id" in ("M"."team_1", "M"."team_2")
  and "PM"."team_id" != "M"."match_winner"

question: Can you help me find the top 5 countries whose players have the highest average of their individual average runs per match across all seasons? Specifically, for each player, calculate their average runs per match over all matches they played, then compute the average of these player averages for each country, and include these country batting averages in the result.
SQL:
WITH Player_Total_Runs AS (
    SELECT 
        bb."striker" AS "player_id",
        SUM(bs."runs_scored") AS "total_runs"
    FROM 
        IPL.IPL.BATSMAN_SCORED bs
    JOIN 
        IPL.IPL.BALL_BY_BALL bb ON bb."match_id" = bs."match_id" AND bb."ball_id" = bs."ball_id" AND bb."innings_no" = bs."innings_no" AND bb."over_id" = bs."over_id"
    GROUP BY 
        bb."striker"
),

Player_Match_Count AS (
    SELECT 
        pm."player_id",
        COUNT(DISTINCT pm."match_id") AS "match_count"
    FROM 
        IPL.IPL.PLAYER_MATCH pm
    GROUP BY 
        pm."player_id"
),

Player_Average_Runs AS (
    SELECT 
        tr."player_id",
        (tr."total_runs" / NULLIF(mc."match_count", 0)) AS "average_runs"
    FROM 
        Player_Total_Runs tr
    JOIN 
        Player_Match_Count mc ON tr."player_id" = mc."player_id"
),

Country_Averages AS (
    SELECT 
        p."country_name",
        AVG(pa."average_runs") AS "country_average_runs"
    FROM 
        Player_Average_Runs pa
    JOIN 
        IPL.IPL.PLAYER p ON pa."player_id" = p."player_id"
    GROUP BY 
        p."country_name"
)

SELECT 
    ca."country_name",
    ca."country_average_runs"
FROM 
    Country_Averages ca
ORDER BY 
    ca."country_average_runs" DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: For each match, considering every innings, please combine runs from both batsman scored and extra runs for each over, then identify the single over with the highest total runs, retrieve the bowler for that over from the ball by ball table, and calculate the average of these highest over totals across all matches, ensuring that all runs and bowler details are accurately reflected.
SQL:
WITH over_runs AS (
    SELECT
        "match_id",
        "innings_no",
        "over_id",
        SUM("runs_scored") AS "batsman_runs"
    FROM IPL.IPL.BATSMAN_SCORED
    GROUP BY "match_id", "innings_no", "over_id"
),
over_extras AS (
    SELECT
        "match_id",
        "innings_no",
        "over_id",
        SUM("extra_runs") AS "extra_runs"
    FROM IPL.IPL.EXTRA_RUNS
    GROUP BY "match_id", "innings_no", "over_id"
),
over_total AS (
    SELECT 
        o_r."match_id",
        o_r."innings_no",
        o_r."over_id",
        o_r."batsman_runs" + COALESCE(o_e."extra_runs", 0) AS "total_runs"
    FROM over_runs o_r
    LEFT JOIN over_extras o_e
        ON o_r."match_id" = o_e."match_id"
        AND o_r."innings_no" = o_e."innings_no"
        AND o_r."over_id" = o_e."over_id"
),
over_bowler AS (
    SELECT
        "match_id",
        "innings_no",
        "over_id",
        MIN("bowler") AS "bowler"
    FROM IPL.IPL.BALL_BY_BALL
    GROUP BY "match_id", "innings_no", "over_id"
),
max_over_per_match_innings AS (
    SELECT
        ot."match_id",
        ot."innings_no",
        ot."over_id",
        ot."total_runs",
        ob."bowler",
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ot."match_id", ot."innings_no" ORDER BY ot."total_runs" DESC, ot."over_id" ASC) AS "rn"
    FROM over_total ot
    JOIN over_bowler ob
        ON ot."match_id" = ob."match_id"
        AND ot."innings_no" = ob."innings_no"
        AND ot."over_id" = ob."over_id"
)
,
max_over_per_match AS (
    SELECT
        "match_id",
        "over_id",
        "innings_no",
        "total_runs",
        "bowler"
    FROM (
        SELECT
            "match_id",
            "over_id",
            "innings_no",
            "total_runs",
            "bowler",
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY "match_id" ORDER BY "total_runs" DESC, "innings_no", "over_id") AS "match_rn"
        FROM max_over_per_match_innings
        WHERE "rn" = 1
    )
    WHERE "match_rn" = 1
)
SELECT
    AVG("total_runs") AS "avg_highest_over_runs"
FROM max_over_per_match;

question: Could you generate a report that shows the number of delivered orders for each month in the years 2016, 2017, and 2018? Each column represents a year, and each row represents a month
SQL:
SELECT 
    CASE delivery_month 
        WHEN 1 THEN 'January'
        WHEN 2 THEN 'February' 
        WHEN 3 THEN 'March'
        WHEN 4 THEN 'April'
        WHEN 5 THEN 'May'
        WHEN 6 THEN 'June'
        WHEN 7 THEN 'July'
        WHEN 8 THEN 'August'
        WHEN 9 THEN 'September'
        WHEN 10 THEN 'October'
        WHEN 11 THEN 'November'
        WHEN 12 THEN 'December'
    END as "Month",
    SUM(CASE WHEN delivery_year = 2016 THEN delivered_orders ELSE 0 END) as "2016",
    SUM(CASE WHEN delivery_year = 2017 THEN delivered_orders ELSE 0 END) as "2017",
    SUM(CASE WHEN delivery_year = 2018 THEN delivered_orders ELSE 0 END) as "2018"
FROM (
    SELECT 
        EXTRACT(YEAR FROM TO_TIMESTAMP("order_delivered_customer_date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')) as delivery_year,
        EXTRACT(MONTH FROM TO_TIMESTAMP("order_delivered_customer_date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')) as delivery_month,
        COUNT(*) as delivered_orders
    FROM BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.OLIST_ORDERS 
    WHERE "order_status" = 'delivered' 
        AND "order_delivered_customer_date" IS NOT NULL 
        AND "order_delivered_customer_date" != ''
        AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM TO_TIMESTAMP("order_delivered_customer_date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')) IN (2016, 2017, 2018)
    GROUP BY delivery_year, delivery_month
) monthly_data
GROUP BY delivery_month
ORDER BY delivery_month

question: What is the highest monthly delivered orders volume in the year with the lowest annual delivered orders volume among 2016, 2017, and 2018?
SQL:
WITH DeliveredOrders AS (
    SELECT
        "order_id",
        TO_CHAR(TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP("order_delivered_customer_date"), 'YYYY-MM') AS "YearMonth",
        TO_CHAR(TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP("order_delivered_customer_date"), 'YYYY') AS "Year"
    FROM
        BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.OLIST_ORDERS
    WHERE
        "order_status" = 'delivered'
        AND TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP("order_delivered_customer_date") IS NOT NULL
        AND TO_CHAR(TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP("order_delivered_customer_date"), 'YYYY') IN ('2016', '2017', '2018')
),
YearlyDeliveredOrders AS (
    SELECT
        "Year",
        COUNT("order_id") AS "YearlyOrderVolume"
    FROM
        DeliveredOrders
    GROUP BY
        "Year"
),
MinimumYear AS (
    SELECT
        "Year"
    FROM
        YearlyDeliveredOrders
    ORDER BY
        "YearlyOrderVolume"
    LIMIT 1
),
MonthlyDeliveredOrders AS (
    SELECT
        "YearMonth",
        COUNT("order_id") AS "MonthlyOrderVolume"
    FROM
        DeliveredOrders
    WHERE
        "Year" = (SELECT "Year" FROM MinimumYear)
    GROUP BY
        "YearMonth"
)
SELECT
    MAX("MonthlyOrderVolume") AS "MaxMonthlyDeliveredOrdersVolume"
FROM
    MonthlyDeliveredOrders;

question: Please identify the top three customers, based on their customer_unique_id, who have the highest number of delivered orders, and provide the average payment value, city, and state for each of these customers.
SQL:
WITH delivered_orders AS (
    SELECT
        o."order_id",
        c."customer_unique_id",
        c."customer_city",
        c."customer_state"
    FROM BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.OLIST_ORDERS o
    JOIN BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.OLIST_CUSTOMERS c
      ON o."customer_id" = c."customer_id"
    WHERE o."order_status" = 'delivered'
),
payment_per_order AS (
    SELECT
        "order_id",
        SUM("payment_value") AS "total_payment_value"
    FROM BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.OLIST_ORDER_PAYMENTS
    GROUP BY "order_id"
),
customer_stats AS (
    SELECT
        d."customer_unique_id",
        COUNT(*)                                AS "delivered_orders",
        AVG(p."total_payment_value")            AS "avg_payment_value",
        MAX(d."customer_city")                  AS "customer_city",
        MAX(d."customer_state")                 AS "customer_state"
    FROM delivered_orders d
    JOIN payment_per_order p
      ON d."order_id" = p."order_id"
    GROUP BY d."customer_unique_id"
)
SELECT
    "customer_unique_id",
    "delivered_orders",
    ROUND("avg_payment_value", 4) AS "avg_payment_value",
    "customer_city",
    "customer_state"
FROM customer_stats
ORDER BY "delivered_orders" DESC, "customer_unique_id"
LIMIT 3;

question: Among all cities with delivered orders, find the five cities whose summed payments are the lowest, then calculate the average of their total payments and the average of their total delivered order counts.
SQL:
WITH CityPayments AS (
  SELECT
    C."customer_city",
    SUM(P."payment_value") AS total_payment,
    COUNT(DISTINCT O."order_id") AS delivered_order_count
  FROM
    "BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE"."BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE"."OLIST_ORDERS" AS O
    JOIN "BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE"."BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE"."OLIST_CUSTOMERS" AS C
      ON O."customer_id" = C."customer_id"
    JOIN "BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE"."BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE"."OLIST_ORDER_PAYMENTS" AS P
      ON O."order_id" = P."order_id"
  WHERE
    O."order_status" = 'delivered'
  GROUP BY
    C."customer_city"
), Top5Cities AS (
  SELECT
    total_payment,
    delivered_order_count
  FROM CityPayments
  ORDER BY
    total_payment ASC
  LIMIT 5
)
SELECT
  AVG(total_payment),
  AVG(delivered_order_count)
FROM Top5Cities;

question: Could you help me find the sellers who excel in the following categories, considering only delivered orders: the seller with the highest number of distinct customer unique IDs, the seller with the highest profit (calculated as price minus freight value), the seller with the highest number of distinct orders, and the seller with the most 5-star ratings? For each category, please provide the seller ID and the corresponding value, labeling each row with a description of the achievement.
SQL:
WITH delivered_orders AS (
    SELECT "order_id"
    FROM BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.OLIST_ORDERS
    WHERE "order_status" = 'delivered'
),
seller_customer_unique_counts AS (
    SELECT
        oi."seller_id",
        COUNT(DISTINCT cu."customer_unique_id") AS num_unique_customers
    FROM
        delivered_orders do
        JOIN BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.OLIST_ORDER_ITEMS oi
            ON do."order_id" = oi."order_id"
        JOIN BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.OLIST_ORDERS o
            ON do."order_id" = o."order_id"
        JOIN BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.OLIST_CUSTOMERS cu
            ON o."customer_id" = cu."customer_id"
    GROUP BY oi."seller_id"
    QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY num_unique_customers DESC) = 1
),
seller_profit AS (
    SELECT
        oi."seller_id",
        ROUND(SUM(oi."price" - oi."freight_value"), 4) AS total_profit
    FROM
        delivered_orders do
        JOIN BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.OLIST_ORDER_ITEMS oi
            ON do."order_id" = oi."order_id"
    GROUP BY oi."seller_id"
    QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY total_profit DESC) = 1
),
seller_order_counts AS (
    SELECT
        oi."seller_id",
        COUNT(DISTINCT oi."order_id") AS num_distinct_orders
    FROM
        delivered_orders do
        JOIN BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.OLIST_ORDER_ITEMS oi
            ON do."order_id" = oi."order_id"
    GROUP BY oi."seller_id"
    QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY num_distinct_orders DESC) = 1
),
seller_five_star_reviews AS (
    SELECT
        oi."seller_id",
        COUNT(DISTINCT rev."order_id") AS num_5star_reviews
    FROM
        delivered_orders do
        JOIN BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.OLIST_ORDER_ITEMS oi
            ON do."order_id" = oi."order_id"
        JOIN BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.OLIST_ORDER_REVIEWS rev
            ON do."order_id" = rev."order_id"
    WHERE rev."review_score" = 5
    GROUP BY oi."seller_id"
    QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY num_5star_reviews DESC) = 1
)

SELECT
    'Most distinct customer unique IDs' AS achievement,
    "seller_id",
    num_unique_customers AS value
FROM seller_customer_unique_counts

UNION ALL

SELECT
    'Highest total profit (price minus freight_value)' AS achievement,
    "seller_id",
    total_profit AS value
FROM seller_profit

UNION ALL

SELECT
    'Most distinct delivered orders' AS achievement,
    "seller_id",
    num_distinct_orders AS value
FROM seller_order_counts

UNION ALL

SELECT
    'Most 5-star reviews' AS achievement,
    "seller_id",
    num_5star_reviews AS value
FROM seller_five_star_reviews
;

question: Could you help me calculate the average of the total number of payments made using the most preferred payment method for each product category, where the most preferred payment method in a category is the one with the highest number of payments?
SQL:
WITH order_categories AS (
    /* 1.  Get each (order, category) combination */
    SELECT DISTINCT
           OI."order_id",
           P."product_category_name"
    FROM  BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.OLIST_ORDER_ITEMS      OI
    JOIN  BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.OLIST_PRODUCTS         P
          ON OI."product_id" = P."product_id"
    WHERE P."product_category_name" IS NOT NULL
),                                                              
category_payments AS (
    /* 2.  Bring the payment information to every category that appears in an order */
    SELECT 
           OC."product_category_name"            AS "category",
           OP."payment_type"
    FROM   order_categories                      OC
    JOIN   BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE.OLIST_ORDER_PAYMENTS  OP
           ON OC."order_id" = OP."order_id"
),                                                               
payment_counts AS (
    /* 3.  Count how many payment records each (category, payment_type) has */
    SELECT 
           "category",
           "payment_type",
           COUNT(*)                              AS "payment_cnt"
    FROM   category_payments
    GROUP  BY "category", "payment_type"
),                                                               
top_payment_per_category AS (
    /* 4.  Keep only the most-used payment_type inside each category */
    SELECT 
           "category",
           "payment_cnt",
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY "category"
                              ORDER BY "payment_cnt" DESC, "payment_type") AS rn
    FROM   payment_counts
)
SELECT 
       ROUND(AVG("payment_cnt"), 4) AS "avg_total_payments_for_top_method"
FROM   top_payment_per_category
WHERE  rn = 1;

question: Identify the top three product categories whose most commonly used payment type has the highest number of payments across all categories, and specify the number of payments made in each category using that payment type.
SQL:
WITH order_items_products AS (
    /* Match every order with the categories of the products it contains.
       Use English category names when available, otherwise keep the original name. */
    SELECT
        oi."order_id",
        COALESCE(t."product_category_name_english",
                 pr."product_category_name")          AS "category"
    FROM "BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE"."BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE"."OLIST_ORDER_ITEMS"    oi
    JOIN "BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE"."BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE"."OLIST_PRODUCTS"       pr
          ON pr."product_id" = oi."product_id"
    LEFT JOIN "BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE"."BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE"."PRODUCT_CATEGORY_NAME_TRANSLATION" t
          ON t."product_category_name" = pr."product_category_name"
    GROUP BY
        oi."order_id",
        COALESCE(t."product_category_name_english", pr."product_category_name")
),
payment_category AS (
    /* Link each (unique) payment record to the categories present in its order.
       DISTINCT avoids double-counting the same payment within a category. */
    SELECT DISTINCT
        p."order_id",
        p."payment_sequential",           -- uniquely identifies a payment record within an order
        p."payment_type",
        oip."category"
    FROM "BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE"."BRAZILIAN_E_COMMERCE"."OLIST_ORDER_PAYMENTS" p
    JOIN order_items_products oip
      ON oip."order_id" = p."order_id"
),
category_top_payment AS (
    /* For each category, find how many times each payment type was used,
       and mark the most frequent payment type per category (rn = 1). */
    SELECT
        "category",
        "payment_type",
        COUNT(*)                                           AS "num_payments",
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY "category"
                           ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC, "payment_type") AS rn
    FROM payment_category
    GROUP BY
        "category",
        "payment_type"
)
/* Return the three categories whose top (most common) payment type
   has the highest number of payments. */
SELECT
    "category"                     AS "product_category",
    "payment_type"                 AS "most_common_payment_type",
    "num_payments"
FROM category_top_payment
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY
    "num_payments" DESC
LIMIT 3;

question: Could you help me determine which actor starred most frequently in English-language children's category films that were rated either G or PG, had a running time of 120 minutes or less, and were released between 2000 and 2010? Please provide the actor's full name.
SQL:
SELECT CONCAT("a"."first_name", ' ', "a"."last_name") AS "actor_full_name"
FROM "PAGILA"."PAGILA"."FILM" AS "f"
JOIN "PAGILA"."PAGILA"."LANGUAGE" AS "l" ON "f"."language_id" = "l"."language_id"
JOIN "PAGILA"."PAGILA"."FILM_CATEGORY" AS "fc" ON "fc"."film_id" = "f"."film_id"
JOIN "PAGILA"."PAGILA"."CATEGORY" AS "c" ON "c"."category_id" = "fc"."category_id"
JOIN "PAGILA"."PAGILA"."FILM_ACTOR" AS "fa" ON "fa"."film_id" = "f"."film_id"
JOIN "PAGILA"."PAGILA"."ACTOR" AS "a" ON "a"."actor_id" = "fa"."actor_id"
WHERE UPPER("c"."name") = 'CHILDREN'
  AND UPPER("l"."name") = 'ENGLISH'
  AND "f"."rating" IN ('G', 'PG')
  AND "f"."length" <= 120
  AND TRY_TO_NUMBER("f"."release_year") BETWEEN 2000 AND 2010
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT "f"."film_id") DESC, "actor_full_name"
LIMIT 1;

question: Please help me find the film category with the highest total rental hours in cities where the city's name either starts with "A" or contains a hyphen.
SQL:
SELECT
  c."name" AS category_name,
  SUM(DATEDIFF('second', TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP_NTZ(r."rental_date"), TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP_NTZ(r."return_date")))/3600.0 AS total_rental_hours
FROM "PAGILA"."PAGILA"."RENTAL" r
JOIN "PAGILA"."PAGILA"."INVENTORY" i ON r."inventory_id" = i."inventory_id"
JOIN "PAGILA"."PAGILA"."FILM_CATEGORY" fc ON i."film_id" = fc."film_id"
JOIN "PAGILA"."PAGILA"."CATEGORY" c ON fc."category_id" = c."category_id"
JOIN "PAGILA"."PAGILA"."CUSTOMER" cu ON r."customer_id" = cu."customer_id"
JOIN "PAGILA"."PAGILA"."ADDRESS" a ON cu."address_id" = a."address_id"
JOIN "PAGILA"."PAGILA"."CITY" ci ON a."city_id" = ci."city_id"
WHERE TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP_NTZ(r."rental_date") IS NOT NULL
  AND TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP_NTZ(r."return_date") IS NOT NULL
  AND (ci."city" ILIKE 'A%' OR ci."city" ILIKE '%-%')
GROUP BY c."category_id", c."name"
ORDER BY total_rental_hours DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: In the combined dataset that unifies the trees data with the income data by ZIP code, filling missing ZIP values where necessary, which three boroughs, restricted to records with median and mean income both greater than zero and a valid borough name, contain the highest number of trees, and what is the average mean income for each of these three boroughs?
SQL:
WITH combined AS (
    SELECT
        t."boroname"                       AS "borough",
        COALESCE(t."zipcode", i."zipcode") AS "zipcode",
        i."Estimate_Mean_income"           AS "mean_income",
        i."Estimate_Median_income"         AS "median_income"
    FROM MODERN_DATA.MODERN_DATA."TREES"         t
    JOIN MODERN_DATA.MODERN_DATA."INCOME_TREES"  i
      ON t."zipcode" = i."zipcode"
    WHERE t."boroname" IS NOT NULL
      AND i."Estimate_Median_income" > 0
      AND i."Estimate_Mean_income"  > 0
)
SELECT
    "borough",
    COUNT(*)                AS "tree_count",
    AVG("mean_income")      AS "average_mean_income"
FROM combined
GROUP BY "borough"
ORDER BY "tree_count" DESC
LIMIT 3;

question: What percentage of trees in the Bronx have a health status of Good?
SQL:
SELECT 
    (COUNT(CASE WHEN "health" = 'Good' THEN 1 END) * 100.0) / COUNT(*) AS percentage_good_health
FROM 
    MODERN_DATA.MODERN_DATA.TREES
WHERE 
    "boroname" = 'Bronx';

question: Can you help me calculate the average number of new unicorn companies per year in the top industry from 2019 to 2021?
SQL:
WITH unicorns_2019_2021 AS (
    SELECT
        d."company_id",
        i."industry",
        YEAR(TRY_TO_DATE(d."date_joined")) AS join_year
    FROM MODERN_DATA.MODERN_DATA.COMPANIES_DATES      d
    JOIN MODERN_DATA.MODERN_DATA.COMPANIES_FUNDING    f  ON d."company_id" = f."company_id"
    JOIN MODERN_DATA.MODERN_DATA.COMPANIES_INDUSTRIES i  ON d."company_id" = i."company_id"
    WHERE TRY_TO_DATE(d."date_joined") BETWEEN '2019-01-01' AND '2021-12-31'
      AND f."valuation" >= 1000000000          -- keep only unicorns (valuation ≥ $1B)
),
industry_rank AS (
    SELECT
        "industry",
        COUNT(DISTINCT "company_id") AS total_unicorns,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT "company_id") DESC) AS rn
    FROM unicorns_2019_2021
    GROUP BY "industry"
),
top_industry AS (
    SELECT "industry"
    FROM industry_rank
    WHERE rn = 1                       -- the industry with the most unicorns 2019-2021
),
per_year AS (
    SELECT
        u.join_year,
        COUNT(DISTINCT u."company_id") AS unicorns_in_year
    FROM unicorns_2019_2021 u
    JOIN top_industry t
      ON u."industry" = t."industry"
    GROUP BY u.join_year
)
SELECT
    SUM(unicorns_in_year)::DECIMAL / 3 AS avg_new_unicorns_per_year
FROM per_year;

question: Could you tell me the first names of customers who spent less than $1 on albums by the best-selling artist, along with the amounts they spent?
SQL:
WITH ArtistSales AS (
    SELECT ar."ArtistId", ar."Name" AS "ArtistName", SUM(il."UnitPrice" * il."Quantity") AS "TotalSales"
    FROM CHINOOK.CHINOOK.ARTISTS ar
    JOIN CHINOOK.CHINOOK.ALBUMS al ON ar."ArtistId" = al."ArtistId"
    JOIN CHINOOK.CHINOOK.TRACKS t ON al."AlbumId" = t."AlbumId"
    JOIN CHINOOK.CHINOOK.INVOICE_ITEMS il ON t."TrackId" = il."TrackId"
    GROUP BY ar."ArtistId", ar."Name"
    ORDER BY "TotalSales" DESC
    LIMIT 1
),
CustomerArtistSpending AS (
    SELECT c."CustomerId", c."FirstName", SUM(il."UnitPrice" * il."Quantity") AS "AmountSpent"
    FROM CHINOOK.CHINOOK.CUSTOMERS c
    JOIN CHINOOK.CHINOOK.INVOICES i ON c."CustomerId" = i."CustomerId"
    JOIN CHINOOK.CHINOOK.INVOICE_ITEMS il ON i."InvoiceId" = il."InvoiceId"
    JOIN CHINOOK.CHINOOK.TRACKS t ON il."TrackId" = t."TrackId"
    JOIN CHINOOK.CHINOOK.ALBUMS al ON t."AlbumId" = al."AlbumId"
    JOIN ArtistSales a ON al."ArtistId" = a."ArtistId"
    GROUP BY c."CustomerId", c."FirstName"
)
SELECT "FirstName", ROUND("AmountSpent", 4) AS "AmountSpent"
FROM CustomerArtistSpending
WHERE "AmountSpent" < 1;

question: Identify the artist with the highest overall sales of albums (tie broken by alphabetical order) and the artist with the lowest overall sales of albums (tie broken by alphabetical order), then calculate the amount each customer spent specifically on those two artists’ albums. Next, compute the average spending for the customers who purchased from the top-selling artist and the average spending for the customers who purchased from the lowest-selling artist, and finally return the absolute difference between these two averages.
SQL:
WITH AlbumSales AS (
    SELECT 
        ALBUMS."ArtistId", 
        SUM(INVOICE_ITEMS."UnitPrice" * INVOICE_ITEMS."Quantity") AS "TotalSales"
    FROM CHINOOK.CHINOOK.INVOICE_ITEMS
    JOIN CHINOOK.CHINOOK.TRACKS ON INVOICE_ITEMS."TrackId" = TRACKS."TrackId"
    JOIN CHINOOK.CHINOOK.ALBUMS ON TRACKS."AlbumId" = ALBUMS."AlbumId"
    GROUP BY ALBUMS."ArtistId"
),
TopArtist AS (
    SELECT ARTISTS."ArtistId", ARTISTS."Name", "TotalSales"
    FROM CHINOOK.CHINOOK.ARTISTS
    JOIN AlbumSales ON ARTISTS."ArtistId" = AlbumSales."ArtistId"
    ORDER BY "TotalSales" DESC, ARTISTS."Name" ASC
    LIMIT 1
),
LowestArtist AS (
    SELECT ARTISTS."ArtistId", ARTISTS."Name", "TotalSales"
    FROM CHINOOK.CHINOOK.ARTISTS
    JOIN AlbumSales ON ARTISTS."ArtistId" = AlbumSales."ArtistId"
    ORDER BY "TotalSales" ASC, ARTISTS."Name" ASC
    LIMIT 1
),
CustomerSpending AS (
    SELECT 
        INVOICES."CustomerId", 
        ALBUMS."ArtistId",
        SUM(INVOICE_ITEMS."UnitPrice" * INVOICE_ITEMS."Quantity") AS "CustomerArtistSpending"
    FROM CHINOOK.CHINOOK.INVOICES
    JOIN CHINOOK.CHINOOK.INVOICE_ITEMS ON INVOICES."InvoiceId" = INVOICE_ITEMS."InvoiceId"
    JOIN CHINOOK.CHINOOK.TRACKS ON INVOICE_ITEMS."TrackId" = TRACKS."TrackId"
    JOIN CHINOOK.CHINOOK.ALBUMS ON TRACKS."AlbumId" = ALBUMS."AlbumId"
    GROUP BY INVOICES."CustomerId", ALBUMS."ArtistId"
),
TopArtistCustomers AS (
    SELECT "CustomerId", "CustomerArtistSpending"
    FROM CustomerSpending
    WHERE "ArtistId" = (SELECT "ArtistId" FROM TopArtist)
),
LowestArtistCustomers AS (
    SELECT "CustomerId", "CustomerArtistSpending"
    FROM CustomerSpending
    WHERE "ArtistId" = (SELECT "ArtistId" FROM LowestArtist)
),
AverageSpending AS (
    SELECT 
        (SELECT AVG("CustomerArtistSpending") FROM TopArtistCustomers) AS "TopArtistAvgSpending",
        (SELECT AVG("CustomerArtistSpending") FROM LowestArtistCustomers) AS "LowestArtistAvgSpending"
)
SELECT 
    ABS("TopArtistAvgSpending" - "LowestArtistAvgSpending") AS "AbsoluteDifference"
FROM AverageSpending;

question: Using the sales data, what is the median value of total sales made in countries where the number of customers is greater than 4?
SQL:
SELECT 
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "total_sales") AS "median_total_sales"
FROM (
    -- total sales per country (only for countries with more than 4 customers)
    SELECT 
        "BillingCountry"                            AS "country",
        SUM("Total")                               AS "total_sales"
    FROM CHINOOK.CHINOOK."INVOICES"
    WHERE "BillingCountry" IN (
        SELECT "Country"
        FROM CHINOOK.CHINOOK."CUSTOMERS"
        GROUP BY "Country"
        HAVING COUNT("CustomerId") > 4
    )
    GROUP BY "BillingCountry"
) t;

question: Which customer has the highest average monthly change in payment amounts? Provide the customer's full name.
SQL:
WITH MonthlyPayments AS (
  SELECT
    "customer_id",
    TO_VARCHAR(TO_TIMESTAMP("payment_date"), 'YYYY-MM') AS "payment_month",
    SUM("amount") AS "monthly_total"
  FROM "SQLITE_SAKILA"."SQLITE_SAKILA"."PAYMENT"
  GROUP BY "customer_id", "payment_month"
), MonthlyChanges AS (
  SELECT
    "customer_id",
    ABS("monthly_total" - LAG("monthly_total", 1) OVER (PARTITION BY "customer_id" ORDER BY "payment_month")) AS "change"
  FROM MonthlyPayments
), AvgMonthlyChange AS (
  SELECT
    "customer_id",
    AVG("change") AS "avg_change"
  FROM MonthlyChanges
  WHERE "change" IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY "customer_id"
)
SELECT
  T1."first_name" || ' ' || T1."last_name"
FROM "SQLITE_SAKILA"."SQLITE_SAKILA"."CUSTOMER" AS T1
JOIN AvgMonthlyChange AS T2
  ON T1."customer_id" = T2."customer_id"
ORDER BY T2."avg_change" DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Can you provide a list of hardware product segments along with their unique product counts for 2020 in the output, ordered by the highest percentage increase in unique fact sales products from 2020 to 2021?
SQL:
WITH UniqueProducts2020 AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT "product_code"
    FROM "EDUCATION_BUSINESS"."EDUCATION_BUSINESS"."HARDWARE_FACT_SALES_MONTHLY"
    WHERE "fiscal_year" = 2020
),
UniqueProducts2021 AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT "product_code"
    FROM "EDUCATION_BUSINESS"."EDUCATION_BUSINESS"."HARDWARE_FACT_SALES_MONTHLY"
    WHERE "fiscal_year" = 2021
),
SegmentProductCount2020 AS (
    SELECT "segment", COUNT(DISTINCT u."product_code") AS "unique_product_count_2020"
    FROM UniqueProducts2020 u
    JOIN "EDUCATION_BUSINESS"."EDUCATION_BUSINESS"."HARDWARE_DIM_PRODUCT" p
    ON u."product_code" = p."product_code"
    GROUP BY "segment"
),
SegmentProductCount2021 AS (
    SELECT "segment", COUNT(DISTINCT u."product_code") AS "unique_product_count_2021"
    FROM UniqueProducts2021 u
    JOIN "EDUCATION_BUSINESS"."EDUCATION_BUSINESS"."HARDWARE_DIM_PRODUCT" p
    ON u."product_code" = p."product_code"
    GROUP BY "segment"
),
PercentageIncrease AS (
    SELECT 
        p2020."segment",
        p2020."unique_product_count_2020",
        p2021."unique_product_count_2021",
        ((p2021."unique_product_count_2021" - p2020."unique_product_count_2020")::FLOAT / NULLIF(p2020."unique_product_count_2020", 0)) * 100 AS "percentage_increase"
    FROM
        SegmentProductCount2020 p2020
    LEFT JOIN SegmentProductCount2021 p2021 ON p2020."segment" = p2021."segment"
)
SELECT 
    "segment",
    "unique_product_count_2020"
FROM 
    PercentageIncrease
ORDER BY 
    "percentage_increase" DESC;

question: For the calendar year 2021, what is the overall average quantity sold of the top three best-selling hardware products (by total quantity sold) in each division?
SQL:
WITH prod_sales AS (
    /* Total quantity sold per product within calendar year 2021 */
    SELECT
        p."division" AS DIVISION,          -- normalize the alias to unquoted, case-insensitive
        f."product_code" AS PRODUCT_CODE,
        SUM(f."sold_quantity") AS TOTAL_QTY
    FROM EDUCATION_BUSINESS.EDUCATION_BUSINESS.HARDWARE_FACT_SALES_MONTHLY f
    JOIN EDUCATION_BUSINESS.EDUCATION_BUSINESS.HARDWARE_DIM_PRODUCT p
        ON f."product_code" = p."product_code"
    WHERE TO_DATE(f."date") BETWEEN '2021-01-01' AND '2021-12-31'
    GROUP BY
        p."division",
        f."product_code"
),
ranked AS (
    /* Identify the top-3 products in each division */
    SELECT
        DIVISION,
        PRODUCT_CODE,
        TOTAL_QTY,
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY DIVISION ORDER BY TOTAL_QTY DESC) AS RNK
    FROM prod_sales
)
SELECT
    DIVISION,
    ROUND(AVG(TOTAL_QTY), 4) AS AVG_QUANTITY_TOP3
FROM ranked
WHERE RNK <= 3                 -- keep only the top 3 products per division
GROUP BY DIVISION
ORDER BY DIVISION;

question: In the United States, for Q4 2019 and Q4 2020, first select only those cities where total sales (with no promotions) rose by at least 20% from Q4 2019 to Q4 2020. Among these cities, rank products by their overall sales (still excluding promotions) in those quarters and take the top 20%. Then compute each top product’s share of total sales in Q4 2019 and Q4 2020 and calculate the difference in share from Q4 2019 to Q4 2020, returning the results in descending order of that share change.
SQL:
WITH
-- Step 1: Find US country_id (fallback if not present in sample, rely on known ISO code)
us_country_id AS (
    SELECT "country_id"
    FROM COMPLEX_ORACLE.COMPLEX_ORACLE.COUNTRIES
    WHERE LOWER("country_iso_code") = 'us'
    LIMIT 1
),

-- Step 2: Get all customers in US
us_customers AS (
    SELECT "cust_id", "cust_city"
    FROM COMPLEX_ORACLE.COMPLEX_ORACLE.CUSTOMERS
    WHERE "country_id" = (SELECT "country_id" FROM us_country_id)
),

-- Step 3: Filter sales with no promotion and join with times for Q4 2019/Q4 2020 and join US customers
filtered_sales AS (
    SELECT
        S."amount_sold",
        S."prod_id",
        S."time_id",
        C."cust_city",
        T."calendar_year",
        T."calendar_quarter_number"
    FROM COMPLEX_ORACLE.COMPLEX_ORACLE.SALES S
    INNER JOIN us_customers C ON S."cust_id" = C."cust_id"
    INNER JOIN COMPLEX_ORACLE.COMPLEX_ORACLE.TIMES T ON S."time_id" = T."time_id"
    WHERE S."promo_id" = 999
        AND T."calendar_year" IN (2019, 2020)
        AND T."calendar_quarter_number" = 4
),

-- Step 4: Aggregate total sales per city per year
city_year_sales AS (
    SELECT
        "cust_city",
        "calendar_year",
        SUM("amount_sold") AS "total_sales"
    FROM filtered_sales
    GROUP BY "cust_city", "calendar_year"
),

-- Step 5: Find cities with at least 20% sales growth from Q4 2019 to 2020
cities_with_growth AS (
    SELECT
        s19."cust_city"
    FROM 
        (SELECT * FROM city_year_sales WHERE "calendar_year" = 2019) s19
        INNER JOIN (SELECT * FROM city_year_sales WHERE "calendar_year" = 2020) s20
        ON s19."cust_city" = s20."cust_city"
    WHERE s19."total_sales" > 0
      AND s20."total_sales" > 0
      AND (s20."total_sales" - s19."total_sales") / s19."total_sales" >= 0.2
),

-- Step 6: Sales only in those cities
sales_growth_cities AS (
    SELECT *
    FROM filtered_sales
    WHERE "cust_city" IN (SELECT "cust_city" FROM cities_with_growth)
),

-- Step 7: Get product aggregate sales for Q4 2019+2020 in these cities
product_total_sales AS (
    SELECT
        "prod_id",
        SUM("amount_sold") AS "total_prod_sales"
    FROM sales_growth_cities
    GROUP BY "prod_id"
),

-- Step 8: Rank products by overall sales and select top 20%
ranked_products AS (
    SELECT
        "prod_id",
        "total_prod_sales",
        NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY "total_prod_sales" DESC) AS ntile_group
    FROM product_total_sales
),
top_products AS (
    SELECT "prod_id"
    FROM ranked_products
    WHERE ntile_group = 1
),

-- Step 9: For top products, compute total sales in Q4 2019 and Q4 2020
top_product_sales_by_year AS (
    SELECT
        "prod_id",
        "calendar_year",
        SUM("amount_sold") AS "year_sales"
    FROM sales_growth_cities
    WHERE "prod_id" IN (SELECT "prod_id" FROM top_products)
    GROUP BY "prod_id", "calendar_year"
),

-- Step 10: Total sales for selected cities for each year
total_sales_by_year AS (
    SELECT
        "calendar_year",
        SUM("amount_sold") AS "total_sales"
    FROM sales_growth_cities
    GROUP BY "calendar_year"
),

-- Step 11: Product's share per year (Q4 only, growth cities only)
product_sales_share AS (
    SELECT
        P."prod_id",
        P."calendar_year",
        ROUND(P."year_sales"/T."total_sales", 4) AS "share"
    FROM top_product_sales_by_year P
    INNER JOIN total_sales_by_year T
      ON P."calendar_year" = T."calendar_year"
),

-- Step 12: Pivot shares, compute difference
product_share_change AS (
    SELECT
        s19."prod_id",
        COALESCE(s19."share", 0) AS "share_2019",
        COALESCE(s20."share", 0) AS "share_2020",
        ROUND(COALESCE(s20."share", 0) - COALESCE(s19."share", 0), 4) AS "share_change"
    FROM
        (SELECT * FROM product_sales_share WHERE "calendar_year" = 2019) s19
    INNER JOIN (SELECT * FROM product_sales_share WHERE "calendar_year" = 2020) s20
      ON s19."prod_id" = s20."prod_id"
)

-- Step 13: Get product names
SELECT
    PSC."prod_id",
    PR."prod_name",
    PSC."share_2019",
    PSC."share_2020",
    PSC."share_change"
FROM product_share_change PSC
INNER JOIN COMPLEX_ORACLE.COMPLEX_ORACLE.PRODUCTS PR
    ON PSC."prod_id" = PR."prod_id"
ORDER BY PSC."share_change" DESC
;
